english: I'm so annoyed I could scream.
alt: This is driving me absolutely nuts.
note: driving me nuts = 미치게 하는 / annoyed = 짜증나는

korean: 이거 리필 되나요?
english: Can I get a refill on this?
alt: Is this free refills?
note: refill = 리필 / free refills = 무료 리필

korean: 올해 장마 언제 시작이래?
english: When does the rainy season start this year?
alt: Any idea when monsoon season kicks off?
note: rainy season / monsoon = 장마 / kick off = 시작되다

korean: 퍼즐 맞추는 거 진짜 재밌어 시간 가는 줄 몰라
english: Doing puzzles is so fun. Time just flies.
alt: I lose track of time doing jigsaw puzzles.
note: time flies / lose track of time = 시간 가는 줄 모르다

korean: 울고 싶으면 울어 참지 마
english: If you need to cry then cry. Don't hold it in.
alt: Let it all out. There's no shame in crying.
note: hold it in = 참다 / let it out = 내보내다

korean: 첫 데이트 어디가 좋을까? 너무 떨려
english: Where should we go for our first date? I'm so nervous.
alt: Any ideas for a first date spot? I'm a bundle of nerves.
note: a bundle of nerves = 매우 긴장한

korean: 이불 빨아야 하는데 날씨가 안 좋네
english: I need to wash the bedding but the weather isn't cooperating.
alt: I should launder the comforter but it doesn't look like good drying weather.
note: bedding / comforter = 이불 / cooperating = 협조하는

korean: 우리 밤새 얘기하자 잠 안 올 것 같아
english: Let's talk all night. I don't think I can sleep anyway.
alt: Let's pull an all-nighter chatting. I'm wide awake.
note: pull an all-nighter = 밤새다 / wide awake = 완전히 깨있는

korean: 이거 교환 가능하나요? 색상이 마음에 안 들어서
english: Can I exchange this? I don't like the color.
alt: Is it possible to swap this out? The color isn't working for me.
note: exchange / swap = 교환하다

korean: 안녕 잘 자 좋은 꿈 꿔
english: Good night! Sleep tight and sweet dreams.
alt: Nighty night! Hope you have good dreams.
note: sleep tight = 잘 자 / sweet dreams = 좋은 꿈 꿔

korean: 조카가 너무 귀여워 보면 힐링돼
english: My niece/nephew is so cute. They're so healing to be around.
alt: My little niece/nephew is adorable. They're my therapy.
note: niece = 조카 (여) / nephew = 조카 (남) / healing = 힐링

korean: 내일 점심 같이 먹을 사람?
english: Anyone down for lunch tomorrow?
alt: Who wants to grab lunch together tomorrow?
note: down for = ~할 의향이 있는 / grab lunch = 점심 먹다

korean: 댓글 달아줘 알고리즘 좀 타게
english: Leave a comment so the algorithm picks it up.
alt: Drop a comment to help boost the engagement.
note: algorithm = 알고리즘 / engagement = 참여도

korean: 비타민 챙겨 먹어 환절기잖아
english: Make sure to take your vitamins. It's the changing of seasons.
alt: Don't forget your vitamins. This is when people get sick.
note: changing of seasons = 환절기 / vitamins = 비타민

korean: 뿌듯하다 열심히 한 보람이 있네
english: I feel so proud. All that hard work paid off.
alt: I'm so satisfied. It was totally worth the effort.
note: paid off = 보상받다 / worth the effort = 노력할 가치가 있는

korean: 이 김치찌개 얼큰하니 딱 좋다
english: This kimchi jjigae is perfectly spicy and warm.
alt: The kick in this kimchi stew is exactly what I needed.
note: kick = 자극적인 맛 / spicy and warm = 얼큰한

korean: 바람 엄청 세다 날아갈 것 같아
english: The wind is insane. I feel like I'm going to blow away.
alt: It's so windy out I can barely stand.
note: blow away = 날아가다 / windy = 바람이 센

korean: 주말에 등산 갈래? 단풍 구경하자
english: Wanna go hiking this weekend? Let's see the fall leaves.
alt: Feel like going on a hike? The autumn foliage should be beautiful.
note: hiking = 등산 / fall leaves / autumn foliage = 단풍

korean: 시간이 약이야 서두르지 마
english: Time heals all wounds. Don't rush it.
alt: Give it time. There's no need to hurry.
note: time heals all wounds = 시간이 약이다

korean: 좋아하는 사람이 생겼어 어떡하지
english: I think I'm developing feelings for someone. What do I do?
alt: I've got a crush on someone. Help me out.
note: develop feelings = 감정이 생기다 / crush = 짝사랑

korean: 오늘 대청소해야겠다 집이 엉망이야
english: I need to do a deep clean today. The house is a mess.
alt: Time for a major cleanup. This place is a disaster.
note: deep clean = 대청소 / mess / disaster = 엉망인

korean: 야 너 진짜 의리 있다
english: You're really loyal. I appreciate that.
alt: You're such a ride-or-die friend.
note: loyal = 의리 있는 / ride-or-die = 죽이 되든 밥이 되든 함께하는

korean: 이거 면세 되나요?
english: Is this duty-free?
alt: Can I get this tax-free?
note: duty-free / tax-free = 면세

korean: 식사하셨어요?
english: Have you eaten yet?
alt: Did you get a chance to eat?
note: Have you eaten은 한국식 안부 인사의 번역

korean: 사촌이 아기 낳았대 축하해줘야지
english: My cousin just had a baby. I should congratulate them.
alt: My cousin's had their baby! I need to send my congrats.
note: had a baby = 아기를 낳다 / congrats = 축하

korean: 토요일 저녁 7시 예약 잡았어
english: I booked a reservation for Saturday at 7 PM.
alt: I've got us a table for 7 on Saturday evening.
note: book a reservation = 예약을 잡다 / got us a table = 자리 잡아놨어

korean: 틱톡에서 완전 핫한 영상 봤어
english: I saw a video that's blowing up on TikTok.
alt: There's this viral TikTok you need to see.
note: blowing up / viral = 핫한 / 바이럴 되는

korean: 목이 부어서 침 삼키기가 힘들어
english: My throat is swollen and it hurts to swallow.
alt: My throat's so sore that even swallowing is painful.
note: swollen = 부은 / swallow = 삼키다

korean: 정말 감사해요 덕분에 많이 배웠어요
english: Thank you so much. I learned a lot thanks to you.
alt: I really appreciate it. You taught me so much.
note: thanks to you = 덕분에 / appreciate = 감사하다

korean: 이거 한 입만 줘봐 맛 좀 볼래
english: Let me have just one bite. I want to try it.
alt: Can I just taste it? Give me a tiny bit.
note: have a bite = 한 입 먹다 / taste = 맛보다

korean: 다음 주부터 진짜 추워진대 패딩 꺼내야겠다
english: I heard it's going to get really cold starting next week. I need to dig out my puffer jacket.
alt: They say it'll be freezing next week. Time to break out the winter coat.
note: puffer jacket / winter coat = 패딩 / dig out = 꺼내다

korean: 요즘 독서에 빠졌어 한 달에 세 권 읽고 있어
english: I've been really into reading lately. I'm reading three books a month.
alt: I've gotten hooked on books. Three a month right now.
note: hooked on = ~에 빠진 / into reading = 독서에 관심 있는

korean: 네 잘못이 아니야 자책하지 마
english: It's not your fault. Don't blame yourself.
alt: This isn't on you. Stop beating yourself up.
note: blame yourself = 자책하다 / beat yourself up = 자신을 탓하다

korean: 장거리 연애 힘들다 빨리 보고 싶어
english: Long-distance is so hard. I want to see you so badly.
alt: Being far apart is tough. I can't wait to be together.
note: long-distance = 장거리 / can't wait = 빨리 ~하고 싶다

korean: 가스비 나왔는데 이번 달 좀 많이 나왔어
english: The gas bill came and it's pretty high this month.
alt: Our gas bill this month is way more than usual.
note: gas bill = 가스비 / way more = 훨씬 많은

korean: 우리 우정 변하지 말자 영원히
english: Let's keep our friendship going forever.
alt: I hope our friendship never changes. Friends for life.
note: friendship = 우정 / friends for life = 평생 친구

korean: 배송비 포함인가요?
english: Does this include shipping?
alt: Is shipping included in the price?
note: shipping = 배송비 / included = 포함된

korean: 오늘도 화이팅! 좋은 하루 보내
english: Have a great day! You got this!
alt: Fighting! Hope your day goes well.
note: you got this = 할 수 있어 / fighting = 화이팅

korean: 외할머니 댁에 이번 주말 가기로 했어
english: We're going to grandma's place on mom's side this weekend.
alt: We decided to visit my maternal grandmother this weekend.
note: maternal grandmother = 외할머니

korean: 미안 그날 선약이 있어 다음에 하자
english: Sorry I already have plans that day. Let's do it next time.
alt: I've got a prior commitment. Rain check?
note: prior commitment = 선약 / rain check = 다음으로 미루자

korean: 유튜브 구독자 천 명 넘었어!
english: I hit 1K subscribers on YouTube!
alt: I just crossed 1000 YouTube subscribers!
note: hit / crossed = 넘었다 / subscribers = 구독자

korean: 소화가 잘 안 돼 더부룩해
english: I'm having trouble digesting. I feel bloated.
alt: My stomach isn't settling. I feel really bloated.
note: bloated = 더부룩한 / digesting = 소화하는

korean: 너무 지루해 뭐 재밌는 거 없나
english: I'm so bored. Is there anything fun to do?
alt: I'm bored out of my mind. Got any ideas?
note: bored out of my mind = 지루해 죽겠다

korean: 여기 디저트도 맛있어? 뭐가 유명해?
english: Is the dessert good here too? What's popular?
alt: Do they have good desserts? What do people usually get?
note: dessert = 디저트 / popular = 인기 있는

korean: 눈이 쌓여서 길이 미끄러워 조심해
english: The snow piled up and the roads are slippery. Be careful.
alt: Watch your step. The roads are icy from all the snow.
note: slippery / icy = 미끄러운 / piled up = 쌓인

korean: 요즘 요가 하는데 유연성이 좋아졌어
english: I've been doing yoga and my flexibility has improved a lot.
alt: Yoga has really helped with my flexibility.
note: flexibility = 유연성 / improved = 향상된

korean: 잘 될 거야 나는 너를 믿어
english: It's going to work out. I believe in you.
alt: You'll be fine. I have faith in you.
note: believe in = ~를 믿다 / have faith = 신뢰하다

korean: 고백할까 말까 고민 중이야
english: I'm debating whether to confess my feelings or not.
alt: I can't decide if I should tell them how I feel.
note: confess = 고백하다 / debating = 고민하는

korean: 창문 열어서 환기 좀 시키자
english: Let's open the windows and air out the room.
alt: We should crack the windows and get some fresh air in here.
note: air out = 환기하다 / crack the windows = 창문을 살짝 열다

korean: 너 아니었으면 나 진짜 큰일 날 뻔했어
english: If it weren't for you I'd have been in big trouble.
alt: You literally saved me back there.
note: if it weren't for you = 네가 아니었으면 / saved me = 살렸어

korean: 이거 최저가 맞나요? 더 깎아주세요
english: Is this the lowest price? Can you give me a better deal?
alt: Can you go any lower? I've seen it cheaper elsewhere.
note: lowest price = 최저가 / go lower = 더 깎다

korean: 다녀오겠습니다!
english: I'm heading out!
alt: I'll be off now!
note: heading out / be off = 나가다

korean: 남동생이 군대 갔어 걱정이 되네
english: My younger brother enlisted in the military. I'm worried about him.
alt: My little brother went off to the army. I can't help but worry.
note: enlisted = 입대하다 / military = 군대

korean: 거기 주차 가능해? 차 가지고 갈까?
english: Is there parking available? Should I drive?
alt: Can I park there? Should I take the car?
note: parking available = 주차 가능 / drive = 차로 가다

korean: DM 보냈는데 확인 좀 해줘
english: I sent you a DM. Check it when you get a chance.
alt: I slid into your DMs. Take a look.
note: DM = 다이렉트 메시지 / slid into DMs = DM을 보내다

korean: 알레르기 때문에 재채기가 멈추질 않아
english: My allergies are making me sneeze nonstop.
alt: I can't stop sneezing because of my allergies.
note: sneeze = 재채기하다 / allergies = 알레르기

korean: 보기만 해도 기분이 좋아져 고마운 사람이야
english: Just seeing you makes me feel better. I'm grateful for you.
alt: You brighten my day just by being around. Thank you.
note: brighten my day = 하루를 밝게 하다 / grateful = 감사한

korean: 먹방 보니까 배고파진다
english: Watching mukbang is making me hungry.
alt: These food videos are giving me serious cravings.
note: mukbang = 먹방 / cravings = 식욕/욕구

korean: 벚꽃 피기 시작했어 구경 가자
english: The cherry blossoms are starting to bloom. Let's go see them.
alt: Cherry blossom season is here! Let's go check them out.
note: cherry blossoms = 벚꽃 / bloom = 피다

korean: 낚시 한번 가볼까? 한 번도 안 해봤는데
english: Should we try fishing? I've never done it before.
alt: Wanna give fishing a shot? It'd be my first time.
note: give it a shot = 한번 해보다 / try fishing = 낚시를 해보다

korean: 다 지나갈 거야 지금이 가장 힘든 거야
english: This will pass. You're going through the hardest part right now.
alt: The worst is almost over. It only gets better from here.
note: this will pass = 다 지나갈 거야 / the worst is over = 가장 힘든 건 끝났다

korean: 기념일인데 뭐 해줄까? 원하는 거 말해
english: It's our anniversary. What can I do for you? Just say the word.
alt: What do you want for our anniversary? Name it.
note: anniversary = 기념일 / say the word / name it = 말만 해

korean: 에어컨 필터 청소해야 되는데 너무 귀찮다
english: I need to clean the AC filter but I really don't want to.
alt: The air conditioner filter needs cleaning. Ugh the thought of it.
note: AC filter = 에어컨 필터 / the thought of it = 생각만 해도

korean: 넌 항상 나를 웃게 해줘서 좋아
english: I love how you always make me laugh.
alt: You always know how to put a smile on my face.
note: make me laugh = 웃게 하다 / put a smile on my face = 미소 짓게 하다

korean: 사은품은 없나요?
english: Are there any freebies with this?
alt: Does this come with any gifts or samples?
note: freebies = 사은품 / samples = 샘플

korean: 밥 먹었어? 같이 먹을래?
english: Have you eaten? Want to grab a bite together?
alt: Did you eat yet? Let's go get some food.
note: grab a bite = 간단히 먹다

korean: 아버지랑 낚시 가기로 했어 오랜만에
english: I'm going fishing with my dad. It's been a while since we hung out.
alt: Dad and I are hitting the lake for some fishing. Long overdue.
note: long overdue = 오래 미룬 / hit the lake = 호수에 가다

korean: 3시에 카페에서 만나자 내가 먼저 가 있을게
english: Let's meet at the café at 3. I'll get there first.
alt: See you at the café at 3. I'll grab us a table.
note: grab a table = 자리를 잡다

english: I ride my bike every weekend and it really relieves stress.
alt: Weekend cycling has been my go-to stress reliever.
note: cycling = 자전거 타기 / stress reliever = 스트레스 해소

note: constantly = 끊임없이 / this is love = 이게 사랑이다

korean: 행주 좀 빨아줄래? 냄새 나기 시작해

alt: The kitchen rags need a wash. They're getting funky.

alt: Wanna switch the location to somewhere more convenient?

english: Is this gluten-free?

english: It's gotten cooler and it's perfect weather for a walk.

english: I signed up for a calligraphy class.

note: signed up / enrolled = 등록하다 / calligraphy = 캘리그라피

korean: 우리 결혼 기념일에 뭐 하고 싶어?

note: wedding anniversary = 결혼 기념일

korean: 세탁기에서 빨래 좀 꺼내줘

note: washing machine = 세탁기 / take out = 꺼내다

korean: 고마워 진짜 든든해

note: solid support = 든든한 지원 / secure = 안심하는

korean: 이거 시착 안 하고 바로 살게요
english: I'll take it without trying it on.
alt: No need to try it on. I'll just buy it.
note: try on = 입어보다 / I'll take it = 살게요

korean: 할아버지 생신이라 온 가족이 모였어

korean: 비 오는 날인데 실내 데이트 하자

note: zero motivation = 의욕이 없는 / unmotivated = 동기부여가 안 되는

note: naengmyeon = 냉면 / cold noodles = 냉면

korean: 폭염주의보 떴대 외출 삼가래

alt: Sleep well. I'll dream about you.
note: see you in my dreams = 꿈에서 보자

korean: 러그 세탁 맡겨야 하는데 비용이 얼마나 할까

alt: We should send the rug to the cleaners. Any idea what they charge?

korean: 야 걔가 그랬다며? 진짜 어이없다
english: He/she really said that? That's ridiculous.
alt: I heard what they did. That's unbelievable.
note: ridiculous / unbelievable = 어이없는

korean: 현금 결제하면 할인 되나요?
english: Is there a cash discount?
alt: Do you give a discount for paying in cash?
note: cash discount = 현금 할인

korean: 오래간만이에요 어떻게 지내세요?
english: It's been so long! How have you been?
alt: Long time! What's new with you?
note: How have you been = 어떻게 지내셨어요

korean: 매형이 이번에 승진했대 축하해줘야지
english: My brother-in-law got promoted. I should congratulate him.
alt: My sister's husband just got a promotion. Great news.
note: brother-in-law = 매형 / promotion = 승진

korean: 코인 노래방 갈래? 스트레스 풀자
english: Wanna go to a coin karaoke? Let's blow off some steam.
alt: Let's hit a karaoke booth and let loose.
note: coin karaoke = 코인노래방 / blow off steam = 스트레스를 풀다

korean: 스토리에 태그 해줘 같이 찍은 사진이잖아
english: Tag me in your story. It's a photo we took together.
alt: Don't forget to tag me on the story since we're both in it.
note: tag = 태그하다 / story = 스토리

korean: 잠을 못 자서 눈이 퉁퉁 부었어
english: I couldn't sleep so my eyes are all puffy.
alt: Lack of sleep has left me with swollen eyes.
note: puffy / swollen = 부은 / lack of sleep = 수면 부족

korean: 긴장돼서 손이 떨려
english: I'm so nervous my hands are shaking.
alt: My hands are trembling from nerves.
note: nervous = 긴장한 / trembling / shaking = 떨리는

korean: 삼겹살에 소주 한잔하자
english: Let's have some pork belly and soju.
alt: How about samgyeopsal with a glass of soju?
note: pork belly = 삼겹살 / soju = 소주

korean: 가을비가 추적추적 내린다 운치 있다
english: It's drizzling autumn rain. There's something romantic about it.
alt: The fall rain is gently pattering down. It's got a nice mood to it.
note: drizzling = 추적추적 내리는 / pattering = 후두둑 떨어지는

korean: 요즘 코딩 배우는 중인데 재밌어
english: I've been learning to code and it's actually fun.
alt: I started picking up programming and I'm really enjoying it.
note: coding / programming = 코딩 / picking up = 배우기 시작하다

korean: 실수는 누구나 해 너무 자책하지 마
english: Everyone makes mistakes. Don't be too hard on yourself.
alt: Mistakes happen. Cut yourself some slack.
note: be hard on yourself = 자책하다 / cut yourself some slack = 여유를 가져

korean: 우리 100일 기념으로 커플링 맞출까?
english: Should we get couple rings for our 100th day?
alt: Want to exchange matching rings for our 100-day anniversary?
note: couple rings = 커플링 / matching rings = 맞춤 반지

korean: 싱크대가 막혔어 업자 불러야겠다
english: The sink is clogged. I need to call a plumber.
alt: The kitchen drain is backed up. Time to call someone.
note: clogged = 막힌 / plumber = 배관공 / backed up = 역류하는

korean: 너랑 있으면 시간이 너무 빨리 가
english: Time flies when I'm with you.
alt: Hours feel like minutes when we hang out.
note: time flies = 시간이 빨리 가다

korean: 이거 사이즈 교환 가능한가요?
english: Can I exchange this for a different size?
alt: Is it possible to swap this for another size?
note: exchange / swap = 교환하다

korean: 감사합니다 좋은 하루 되세요
english: Thank you. Have a wonderful day.
alt: Thanks so much. I hope you have a great day.
note: Have a wonderful day = 좋은 하루 되세요

korean: 둘째가 첫걸음 뗐어! 감동이야
english: Our second child took their first steps! I'm so moved.
alt: The baby just started walking! What a moment!
note: first steps = 첫걸음 / moved = 감동받은

korean: 헬스장에서 만날까? 같이 운동하자
english: Want to meet at the gym? Let's work out together.
alt: Let's hit the gym together.
note: gym = 헬스장 / work out = 운동하다

korean: 이 계정 팔로우해봐 콘텐츠 진짜 좋아

alt: You should check out this page. The content is top-notch.
note: follow = 팔로우하다 / top-notch = 최고의

alt: This migraine is killing my concentration.
note: migraine = 편두통 / focus / concentration = 집중

alt: This has got to be the crappiest day I've had.
note: worst day ever = 최악의 하루 / crappy = 최악인

korean: 여기 빵이 유명하대 먹어볼래?

alt: This bakery is supposed to be the best. Shall we give it a go?
note: famous for = ~로 유명한 / give it a go = 한번 해보다

korean: 한여름인데 시원한 바람이 불어서 다행이다

alt: Midsummer but we've got a nice cool wind. Lucky us.

korean: 요리 유튜브 보면서 새 레시피 도전했어

alt: I followed along with a cooking video and made something new.
note: recipe = 레시피 / followed along = 따라서 했다

alt: All that effort is bound to lead to good results.
note: pay off = 보상받다 / bound to = 반드시 ~할

alt: I love you. My feelings just keep growing.
note: love = 사랑 / feelings keep growing = 감정이 계속 커지는

alt: The bulb needs replacing but I'm too short to reach.
note: light bulb = 전구 / reach = 닿다

korean: 야 졸업 축하해! 진짜 대단하다
english: Congrats on graduating! That's a huge accomplishment.
alt: Way to go on your graduation! So proud of you.
note: congrats / way to go = 축하해 / accomplishment = 성취

korean: 영수증 필요하세요?
english: Do you need the receipt?
alt: Would you like a receipt?
note: receipt = 영수증

korean: 들어오세요 어서 오세요!
english: Come on in! Welcome!
alt: Please come in! Make yourself at home!
note: come on in = 들어오세요 / make yourself at home = 편히 쉬세요

korean: 어머니가 건강검진 받으셔야 하는데 같이 가야겠다

alt: Mom's due for a physical. I'll accompany her.
note: health checkup / physical = 건강검진 / accompany = 동행하다

korean: 내일 저녁 약속 취소해야 할 것 같아 미안
english: I think I need to cancel our dinner plans for tomorrow. Sorry.
alt: I'm going to have to bail on tomorrow night. My bad.
note: cancel / bail on = 취소하다 / my bad = 미안

korean: 브이로그 찍으려고 하는데 카메라 추천해줘

alt: I want to start a vlog. Any camera suggestions?
note: vlog = 브이로그 / recommend = 추천하다

alt: I've got back pain. It's probably from poor posture.
note: posture = 자세 / back pain = 등/허리 통증

english: I need a change of pace. Should I go somewhere?
alt: I need to clear my head. Maybe I should get out for a bit.
note: change of pace = 기분 전환 / clear my head = 머리를 비우다

korean: 이거 비건 메뉴 있나요?
