alt: Is there anything on the menu that's vegan-friendly?
note: vegan options = 비건 메뉴 / vegan-friendly = 비건 친화적

korean: 봄바람이 살랑살랑 불어서 좋다
english: The spring breeze is gently blowing. It feels lovely.
alt: There's a soft spring wind and it's just wonderful.
note: spring breeze = 봄바람 / gently = 살랑살랑

korean: 이번 주말에 전시회 갈까? 재밌어 보여
english: Want to go to an exhibition this weekend? It looks interesting.
alt: There's a cool exhibit this weekend. Wanna check it out?
note: exhibition / exhibit = 전시회 / check it out = 가보다

korean: 잘 안 될 때도 있어 쉬어가도 괜찮아
english: Things don't always go well. It's okay to take a break.
alt: Not every day is a win. Resting is part of the process.
note: take a break = 쉬다 / part of the process = 과정의 일부

korean: 오늘 우리 만난 날이야 잊지 않았지?
english: Today's the day we first met. You didn't forget right?
alt: It's our anniversary of meeting. Please tell me you remembered.
note: anniversary = 기념일 / remembered = 기억한

korean: 곰팡이 생겼어 습기 제거 좀 해야겠다
english: There's mold growing. I need to deal with the moisture.
alt: Mold's starting to appear. We need better ventilation.
note: mold = 곰팡이 / moisture = 습기 / ventilation = 환기

korean: 야 너 진짜 변하지 않았다 그대로야
english: You haven't changed one bit. Still the same.
alt: You're exactly the same as I remember.
note: haven't changed = 변하지 않은 / exactly the same = 그대로인

korean: 이거 예쁜데 선물 포장 해주세요
english: This is pretty. Could you gift-wrap it please?
alt: I love this one. Can I get it wrapped?
note: gift-wrap = 선물 포장하다 / pretty = 예쁜

korean: 축하해! 잘 됐다!
english: Congratulations! That's great news!
alt: Way to go! I'm so happy for you!
note: congratulations = 축하해 / way to go = 잘했어

korean: 고모가 오셨어 오랜만에 뵈니까 반갑다
english: My aunt came to visit. It's so nice to see her after such a long time.
alt: Auntie's here! It's been ages since we last saw her.
note: aunt = 고모 / it's been ages = 오래됐다

korean: 약속 시간에 딱 맞춰서 왔어 안 늦었지?
english: I made it right on time. I'm not late am I?
alt: I got here just in the nick of time. Not late right?
note: right on time = 딱 맞춰서 / in the nick of time = 아슬아슬하게

korean: 사진 보정 앱 뭐 써? 추천 좀
english: What photo editing app do you use? Any recommendations?
alt: Which app do you edit your photos with? Got any good ones?
note: photo editing app = 사진 보정 앱

korean: 혈압이 높다고 하니까 짜게 먹지 마
english: Your blood pressure is high so lay off the salty food.
alt: Since your BP is elevated you should cut back on sodium.
note: blood pressure = 혈압 / lay off = 줄이다 / sodium = 나트륨

korean: 진짜 억울해 내가 한 게 아닌데
english: I feel so wronged. I didn't even do it.
alt: This is so unfair. It wasn't me.
note: wronged / unfair = 억울한

korean: 여기 런치 세트 있어요? 가성비 좋은 거로
english: Do you have a lunch set? Something that's good value.
alt: Is there a lunch combo? I'm looking for something affordable.
note: lunch set / combo = 런치 세트 / good value = 가성비 좋은

korean: 날이 흐려서 우울하다
english: It's cloudy and it's making me feel gloomy.
alt: Overcast days always bring down my mood.
note: cloudy / overcast = 흐린 / gloomy = 우울한

korean: 드로잉 클래스 다니는데 실력이 늘고 있어
english: I'm taking a drawing class and my skills are improving.
alt: I've been going to drawing classes and I'm getting better.
note: drawing class = 드로잉 클래스 / improving = 향상되는

korean: 아무리 힘들어도 포기만은 하지 마
english: No matter how hard it gets just don't give up.
alt: However tough things are never throw in the towel.
note: give up / throw in the towel = 포기하다

korean: 너한테 할 말이 있어 만나서 얘기하자
english: I have something to tell you. Let's talk in person.
alt: There's something I need to say. Can we meet up?
note: talk in person = 만나서 얘기하다

korean: 보일러 점검 받아야 할 때야 겨울 전에
english: We should get the boiler inspected before winter.
alt: Time for a boiler checkup before the cold hits.
note: boiler = 보일러 / inspected = 점검받는

korean: 야 너 생일 파티 해야지 내가 준비할게
english: We should throw you a birthday party. I'll plan it.
alt: You deserve a birthday bash. Leave the planning to me.
note: throw a party = 파티를 열다 / bash = 파티

korean: 이거 신제품이에요? 언제 출시됐어요?
english: Is this a new product? When was it released?
alt: Is this newly launched? What's the release date?
note: new product = 신제품 / released / launched = 출시된

korean: 만나서 반가웠어 또 보자
english: It was great meeting you. Let's do this again.
alt: Nice seeing you. We should catch up again soon.
note: nice seeing you = 반가웠어 / catch up = 다시 만나다

korean: 아빠가 퇴직하셨는데 뭐 하시려나
english: My dad retired. I wonder what he's going to do.
alt: Dad's retired now. Not sure what his plans are.
note: retired = 퇴직한 / plans = 계획

korean: 주차장에서 만나서 같이 가자
english: Let's meet in the parking lot and go together.
alt: I'll pick you up at the parking garage and we'll head out.
note: parking lot = 주차장 / head out = 출발하다

korean: 오늘 올린 릴스 조회수가 터졌어
english: My reel today went viral.
alt: The views on my reel today just exploded.
note: went viral = 바이럴 됐다 / exploded = 폭발했다

korean: 발목을 삐었어 걷기가 힘들어
english: I twisted my ankle. It's hard to walk.
alt: I sprained my ankle and walking is painful.
note: twisted / sprained = 삐다 / ankle = 발목

korean: 아 진짜 감격스러워 눈물 나올 것 같아
english: I'm so overwhelmed with emotion. I could cry.
alt: This is so touching I'm about to tear up.
note: overwhelmed = 벅찬 / touching = 감동적인

korean: 이거 리필 무한인가요?
english: Is this unlimited refills?
alt: Can I get as many refills as I want?
note: unlimited refills = 무한리필

korean: 오늘 아침 안개 엄청 끼었다 앞이 안 보여
english: There's so much fog this morning. I can barely see ahead.
alt: The morning fog is insane. Visibility is practically zero.
note: fog = 안개 / visibility = 가시거리

korean: 주말에 볼링 치러 갈래?
english: Want to go bowling this weekend?
alt: How about we hit the bowling alley on Saturday?
note: bowling = 볼링 / bowling alley = 볼링장

korean: 세상이 끝난 것처럼 느껴져도 내일은 올 거야
english: Even if it feels like the end of the world tomorrow will still come.
alt: When everything seems dark remember that a new day always dawns.
note: end of the world = 세상 끝 / a new day dawns = 새 날이 밝는다

korean: 너만 보면 심장이 막 뛰어
english: My heart starts racing whenever I see you.
alt: I get butterflies every time you're around.
note: heart racing = 심장이 빨리 뛰는 / butterflies = 두근거림

korean: 이사할 때 짐 좀 도와줘
english: Help me move my stuff when I relocate.
alt: Can you lend a hand with the move?
note: relocate / move = 이사하다 / lend a hand = 도와주다

korean: 걔 뒷담 하지 마 안 좋아
english: Don't talk behind their back. It's not cool.
alt: Let's not gossip about them. It's not right.
note: talk behind someone's back = 뒷담화하다 / gossip = 험담

korean: 이거 세트로 사면 더 싼가요?
english: Is it cheaper if I buy the set?
alt: Do I get a better deal buying the bundle?
note: set / bundle = 세트 / better deal = 더 좋은 거래

korean: 어서 와 기다리고 있었어
english: Welcome! I've been waiting for you.
alt: Come in! I was expecting you.
note: waiting for you = 기다리고 있었어 / expecting = 기다리고 있었다

korean: 여동생이 유학 가서 보고 싶어
english: My younger sister went abroad to study and I miss her.
alt: My little sis is studying overseas. I really miss her.
note: study abroad = 유학 / miss = 보고 싶다

korean: 거기 예약 필수야? 아니면 그냥 가도 돼?
english: Do we need a reservation or can we just walk in?
alt: Is it reservations only or can we show up without one?
note: walk in / show up = 그냥 가다 / reservation = 예약

korean: 셀카 찍자 여기 배경 예쁘다
english: Let's take a selfie. The backdrop here is beautiful.
alt: Selfie time! This background is stunning.
note: selfie = 셀카 / backdrop / background = 배경

korean: 어제 백신 맞았는데 팔이 아파
english: I got vaccinated yesterday and my arm is sore.
alt: My arm's been hurting since getting my shot yesterday.
note: vaccinated / shot = 백신 / sore = 아픈

korean: 기분이 묘해 좋은 건지 슬픈 건지 모르겠어
english: I feel weird. I can't tell if I'm happy or sad.
alt: I'm in a strange mood. It's a mix of emotions.
note: weird / strange = 묘한 / mix of emotions = 감정이 섞인

korean: 이거 테이블에서 불판 구워 먹는 거예요?
english: Is this the kind you grill at the table?
alt: Do you cook this on a tabletop grill?
note: grill = 구이 / tabletop grill = 테이블 불판

korean: 해 질 때 하늘이 분홍색이야 진짜 예쁘다
english: The sky is pink at sunset. It's so beautiful.
alt: What a gorgeous pink sky at dusk.
note: sunset / dusk = 해질녘 / pink sky = 분홍 하늘

korean: 방탈출 해볼래? 재밌다고 하더라
english: Want to try an escape room? I heard it's fun.
alt: How about an escape room? People say they're a blast.
note: escape room = 방탈출 / a blast = 엄청 재미있는

korean: 실패가 끝이 아니야 새로운 시작이야
english: Failure isn't the end. It's a new beginning.
alt: A setback is just a setup for a comeback.
note: failure = 실패 / new beginning = 새로운 시작 / comeback = 재기

korean: 첫사랑이 잊히질 않아
english: I can't get over my first love.
alt: My first love still lingers in my mind.
note: first love = 첫사랑 / can't get over = 잊을 수 없다 / lingers = 맴도는

korean: 택배 왔는데 좀 받아줄 수 있어?
english: A package came. Can you pick it up for me?
alt: I've got a delivery coming. Mind grabbing it?
note: package / delivery = 택배 / pick up / grab = 받다

korean: 야 우리 동창회 가자 이번에는 꼭!
english: Let's go to the class reunion this time for sure!
alt: We have to make it to the alumni meetup this time!
note: class reunion / alumni meetup = 동창회

korean: 온라인으로 주문할게요 배송 며칠 걸려요?
english: I'll order it online. How many days does shipping take?
alt: I'll buy it online. What's the delivery time?
note: shipping / delivery = 배송 / order = 주문하다

korean: 생신 축하드립니다! 건강하세요
english: Happy birthday! Stay healthy.
alt: Wishing you a very happy birthday and good health.
note: happy birthday = 생신 축하 / stay healthy = 건강하세요

korean: 장인어른이 골프 치자고 하셔서 긴장돼
english: My father-in-law invited me to play golf and I'm nervous.
alt: The in-laws want to go golfing. I'm a bit stressed about it.
note: father-in-law = 장인어른 / golf = 골프

korean: 약속 잡았으면 꼭 지켜 바람 맞히지 마
english: If you make a promise keep it. Don't stand me up.
alt: When you commit to plans follow through. No flaking.
note: stand someone up = 바람 맞히다 / flaking = 약속을 어기는

korean: 언팔하지 마 서운하게
english: Don't unfollow me. That would hurt my feelings.
alt: If you unfollow me I'll be offended.
note: unfollow = 언팔하다 / offended = 서운한

korean: 비타민D 부족이래 햇볕 좀 쬐야겠다
english: They said I'm vitamin D deficient. I need to get some sun.
alt: I'm low on vitamin D. Time to soak up some sunshine.
note: vitamin D deficient = 비타민D 부족 / soak up = 쬐다

korean: 너무 신나서 가만히 못 있겠어
english: I'm so excited I can't sit still.
alt: I'm buzzing with excitement. I can't contain myself.
note: can't sit still = 가만히 못 있다 / buzzing = 흥분한

korean: 후식으로 뭐 먹을래? 커피? 아이스크림?
english: What do you want for dessert? Coffee? Ice cream?
alt: What should we have after the meal? Something sweet?
note: dessert = 후식 / after the meal = 식후

korean: 태풍 온대 창문 잠갔어?
english: I heard a typhoon is coming. Did you lock the windows?
alt: There's a typhoon warning. Are the windows secured?
note: typhoon = 태풍 / lock / secure = 잠그다

korean: 주말에 플리마켓 구경 갈래?
english: Want to check out a flea market this weekend?
alt: Let's browse the weekend flea market.
note: flea market = 플리마켓 / browse = 구경하다

korean: 비교하지 마 너는 너야 충분히 잘하고 있어
english: Don't compare yourself to others. You are you and you're doing great.
alt: Stop measuring yourself against everyone else. You're doing amazing.
note: compare = 비교하다 / measuring yourself = 자신을 재다

korean: 네가 행복하면 나도 행복해
english: When you're happy I'm happy too.
alt: Your happiness is my happiness.
note: happiness = 행복

korean: 가구 조립하는데 나사가 하나 부족해
english: I'm assembling furniture and I'm one screw short.
alt: I'm putting together this furniture but there's a missing screw.
note: assembling = 조립하는 / screw = 나사

korean: 야 너 요즘 뭐 하고 사니? 바빠?
english: What have you been up to these days? Busy?
alt: What's going on in your life? You've been MIA.
note: what have you been up to = 요즘 뭐 해? / MIA = 연락이 안 되는

korean: 이거 반품 기한이 언제까지예요?
english: What's the return deadline for this?
alt: How long do I have to return this?
note: return deadline = 반품 기한

korean: 잘 부탁드립니다!
english: I look forward to working with you!
alt: Please take good care of me!
note: I look forward to working with you = 잘 부탁드립니다

korean: 명절에 가족끼리 윷놀이 하자
english: Let's play Yut Nori with the family during the holidays.
alt: How about a round of Yut Nori at the family gathering?
note: Yut Nori = 윷놀이 / family gathering = 가족 모임

korean: 약속 잡으려면 일주일 전에는 말해줘
english: If you want to make plans give me at least a week's notice.
alt: I need a heads up at least a week in advance to lock in plans.
note: a week's notice = 일주일 전 통보 / lock in = 확정하다

korean: 팔로우 맞팔 해줄게
english: I'll follow you back.
alt: I'll give you a follow back.
note: follow back = 맞팔하다

korean: 코막힘이 심해서 숨쉬기가 힘들어
english: My nose is so congested it's hard to breathe.
alt: I'm so stuffed up I can barely breathe through my nose.
note: congested / stuffed up = 코가 막힌

korean: 오랜만에 행복한 하루였어
english: It's been a genuinely happy day for the first time in a while.
alt: I haven't felt this happy in a long time.
note: for the first time in a while = 오랜만에

korean: 이 집 순두부찌개가 일품이야
english: The sundubu-jjigae at this place is exceptional.
alt: Their soft tofu stew is out of this world.
note: exceptional / out of this world = 일품인 / 최고인

korean: 오늘 최고기온 35도래 찜통이겠다
english: The high today is 35 degrees. It's going to be a sauna.
alt: It'll hit 35 today. We're looking at an absolute scorcher.
note: sauna / scorcher = 찜통 / 무더운 날

korean: 집에서 홈카페 차리는 거 완전 빠졌어
english: I'm totally into setting up a home café.
alt: I've been obsessed with making café-quality drinks at home.
note: home café = 홈카페 / café-quality = 카페 수준의

korean: 지금 당장은 아무것도 안 보여도 길은 반드시 있어
english: Even if you can't see a way right now there's always a path.
alt: It might feel like a dead end but trust me there's a way forward.
note: path = 길 / dead end = 막다른 길 / way forward = 나아갈 길

korean: 너 없는 하루는 의미가 없어
english: A day without you is meaningless.
alt: Nothing feels right when you're not here.
note: meaningless = 의미 없는 / nothing feels right = 아무것도 맞지 않는

korean: 세제 떨어졌어 마트 갈 때 좀 사다 줘
english: We're out of detergent. Pick some up when you go to the store.
alt: We ran out of laundry soap. Grab some from the mart.
note: detergent = 세제 / out of / ran out = 떨어진

korean: 야 너 진짜 약속 잘 지킨다 멋있어
english: You always keep your promises. That's admirable.
alt: I love how you're always true to your word.
note: keep promises = 약속을 지키다 / true to your word = 약속에 충실한

korean: 포장지 빼주세요 바로 쓸 거예요
english: No need for wrapping. I'm going to use it right away.
alt: Skip the packaging please. I'll use it as is.
note: wrapping / packaging = 포장 / as is = 그대로

korean: 고생했어 오늘도!
english: Great work today!
alt: You crushed it today!
note: great work / crushed it = 잘했어

korean: 외삼촌이 사업 시작하셨대 응원해야지
english: My uncle on my mom's side started a business. I should support him.
alt: My maternal uncle launched a business. Gotta cheer him on.
note: maternal uncle = 외삼촌 / launched = 시작한

korean: 금요일 저녁에 회식 있어 참석할 수 있어?
english: We have a company dinner Friday night. Can you make it?
alt: There's a team dinner on Friday. Are you in?
note: company dinner = 회식 / make it = 참석하다 / are you in = 할래?

korean: 게시물 저장해놨어 나중에 다시 볼게
english: I saved your post. I'll check it out again later.
alt: I bookmarked your post for later.
note: saved / bookmarked = 저장했다

korean: 면역력이 떨어진 것 같아 건강관리 해야겠다
english: I think my immune system is weakened. I need to take better care of myself.
alt: My immunity feels low. Time to focus on my health.
note: immune system / immunity = 면역력 / weakened = 약해진

korean: 너무 불안해서 아무것도 손에 안 잡혀
english: I'm so anxious that I can't focus on anything.
alt: My anxiety is through the roof. I can't do anything productive.
note: anxious / anxiety = 불안 / through the roof = 극에 달한

korean: 여기 주차 발렛 되나요?
english: Do you offer valet parking here?
alt: Is valet parking available?
note: valet parking = 발렛파킹

korean: 아침에 일어나기 싫다 5분만 더
english: I don't want to get up in the morning. Just five more minutes.
alt: I can't get out of bed. Give me five more minutes.
note: five more minutes = 5분만 더 / get out of bed = 일어나다

korean: 저 사람 완전 내 이상형이야
english: That person is totally my type.
alt: They're exactly what I've been looking for.
note: my type = 내 이상형 / what I've been looking for = 내가 찾던

korean: 집 앞에 꽃 심으려고 화분 샀어
english: I bought a pot to plant flowers in front of the house.
alt: I picked up a planter to put some flowers outside.
note: pot / planter = 화분 / plant = 심다

korean: 이번 연휴에 뭐 할 거야? 여행 갈까?
english: What are you doing this holiday? Should we travel?
alt: Got any plans for the break? Wanna go on a trip?
note: holiday / break = 연휴 / travel / trip = 여행

korean: 오늘 미세먼지 좋음이래 창문 열자
english: Air quality is good today. Let's open the windows.
alt: Fine dust levels are low today. Great day to air out the place.
note: air quality = 공기질 / air out = 환기하다

korean: 모든 게 엉망인 것 같아서 답답해
english: I feel frustrated because everything seems to be falling apart.
alt: Everything's a mess and I feel suffocated.
note: falling apart = 무너지는 / frustrated = 답답한 / suffocated = 숨막히는

korean: 오늘 점심은 내가 쏜다 골라
english: Lunch is on me today. Pick whatever you want.
alt: I'm treating you to lunch. You choose.
note: on me / treating = 내가 사는 / pick = 골라

korean: 설 연휴에 떡국 먹어야 나이 한 살 먹지
english: You have to eat tteokguk during Lunar New Year to age a year.
alt: Eating rice cake soup on New Year's is how you officially turn a year older.
note: tteokguk = 떡국 / Lunar New Year = 설날

korean: 너무 고마워서 뭐라고 해야 할지 모르겠어
english: I'm so grateful I don't even know what to say.
alt: Words can't express how thankful I am.
note: grateful / thankful = 감사한 / words can't express = 말로 표현할 수 없다

korean: 이 노래 누가 부른 거야? 좋다
english: Who sings this song? I love it.
alt: What's this track? It's a banger.
note: who sings this = 누가 불렀어 / banger = 좋은 노래

korean: 날이 좀 풀렸다 이제 겨울 끝인가 봐
english: The weather has warmed up a bit. I think winter is finally ending.
alt: It's getting milder. Looks like winter is on its way out.
note: warmed up = 풀린 / milder = 더 따뜻한 / on its way out = 끝나가는

korean: 그건 네가 결정할 문제야 네 인생이니까
english: That's your call. It's your life after all.
alt: Only you can make that decision. It's your life.
note: your call = 네 결정 / it's your life = 네 인생이야

korean: 형이 군대 전역했어 드디어 나왔다
english: My older brother was discharged from the military. He's finally out.
alt: My bro is done with his service! He's finally free.
note: discharged = 전역한 / military service = 군복무

korean: 그 식당 웨이팅 얼마나 걸려?
english: How long is the wait at that restaurant?
alt: What's the wait time like at that place?
note: wait / wait time = 대기 시간

korean: 프로필 소개글 뭐라고 쓸지 고민이야
english: I can't figure out what to write in my bio.
alt: I'm stuck on what to put in my profile description.
note: bio = 소개글 / profile description = 프로필 설명

korean: 다리가 저려서 못 일어나겠어
english: My legs are numb. I can't get up.
alt: My legs fell asleep and I can't stand up.
note: numb / fell asleep = 저린

korean: 너 정말 잘 어울린다 그 옷
english: That outfit looks really good on you.
alt: You're rocking that look.
note: looks good on you = 잘 어울린다 / rocking = 멋지게 소화하는

korean: 택시 부를까? 걸어가기엔 좀 멀어
english: Should we call a taxi? It's a bit far to walk.
alt: Let's grab a cab. Walking there would take too long.
note: call a taxi / grab a cab = 택시 부르다

korean: 귀찮지만 해야 할 일이 산더미야
english: I don't want to but I've got a mountain of things to do.
alt: I'm not motivated but my to-do list is endless.
note: mountain of things = 산더미같은 일 / to-do list = 할 일 목록

korean: 같이 있어줘서 고마워 혼자였으면 힘들었을 거야
english: Thanks for being here. I would've struggled alone.
alt: I appreciate your company. It would've been tough on my own.
note: company = 함께함 / struggled = 힘들었을

korean: 새 폰 샀어? 사진 퀄리티 미쳤다
english: Did you get a new phone? The photo quality is insane.
alt: New phone? The camera quality is next level.
note: photo quality = 사진 퀄리티 / next level = 다음 레벨의

korean: 피부가 건조해서 보습제 발라야겠다
english: My skin is so dry. I need to apply moisturizer.
alt: I've got dry skin. Time to slather on some lotion.
note: moisturizer = 보습제 / dry skin = 건조한 피부

korean: 야 진짜 부럽다 나도 그러고 싶어
english: I'm so jealous. I want to do that too.
alt: Lucky you! I wish I could do the same.
note: jealous = 부러운 / lucky you = 운 좋네

korean: 이 집 김밥이 진짜 맛있어 한줄 더 시킬까?
english: The gimbap here is incredible. Should we order another roll?
alt: Their kimbap is the bomb. Let's get one more.
note: gimbap / kimbap = 김밥 / the bomb = 최고인

korean: 아침에 서리 내렸어 완전 추워졌다
english: There was frost this morning. It's gotten really cold.
alt: Jack Frost visited this morning. Winter's setting in.
note: frost = 서리 / setting in = 본격적으로 시작되는

korean: 레고 조립하는 거 진짜 중독성 있어
english: Building LEGO sets is seriously addictive.
alt: I'm hooked on assembling LEGO. Can't stop.
note: addictive = 중독성 있는 / hooked on = ~에 빠진

korean: 천천히 가도 괜찮아 네 속도대로 해
english: It's okay to go slow. Do it at your own pace.
alt: There's no rush. Take things at your speed.
note: at your own pace = 네 속도대로 / no rush = 급할 거 없어

korean: 이별 후에 더 성장할 거야 분명
english: You'll grow so much after this breakup. I'm sure of it.
alt: Breakups make you stronger. You'll come out better.
note: breakup = 이별 / grow = 성장하다

korean: 냉장고 정리 좀 하자 유통기한 지난 것들 버려야 해
english: Let's clean out the fridge. We need to toss expired stuff.
alt: The fridge needs organizing. Some things are past their expiration date.
note: expired = 유통기한 지난 / toss = 버리다

korean: 야 너 요즘 연애하냐? 얼굴에 다 써있어
english: Are you seeing someone? It's written all over your face.
alt: You're dating someone aren't you? I can tell by your face.
note: seeing someone = 누군가를 만나는 / written all over your face = 얼굴에 써있는

korean: 적립 카드 만들까요?
english: Would you like to sign up for a rewards card?
alt: Do you want me to set up a points card for you?
note: rewards card / points card = 적립 카드

korean: 건강하게 잘 지내 다음에 또 봐
english: Stay healthy. See you next time.
alt: Take care of yourself. Let's meet again soon.
note: stay healthy = 건강하게 지내 / take care = 잘 지내

korean: 처가댁 갈 때 뭐 사가야 하지
english: What should I bring when visiting my in-laws?
alt: I need to pick something up for my wife's parents' house.
note: in-laws = 처가 / bring = 가져가다

korean: 주말에 바비큐 파티 할까? 우리 집에서
english: Want to have a BBQ party at my place this weekend?
alt: Let's throw a barbecue at mine this weekend.
note: BBQ / barbecue = 바비큐 / at my place = 우리 집에서

korean: 이 사진 태그 빼줘 내가 너무 못 나왔어
english: Remove my tag from this photo. I look terrible.
alt: Untag me from that pic. I'm not photogenic in it.
note: untag = 태그 해제 / photogenic = 사진이 잘 받는

korean: 밤새 기침해서 목이 다 쉬었어
english: I coughed all night and now my voice is hoarse.
alt: My throat is raw from coughing all night long.
note: hoarse = 쉰 / raw = 아픈 / coughing = 기침하는

korean: 와 진짜 대박이다 믿을 수가 없어
english: Wow that's amazing! I can't believe it!
alt: This is unreal! I'm blown away!
note: amazing / unreal = 대박인 / blown away = 놀란

korean: 밥 위에 계란 프라이 올리면 꿀맛이야
english: A fried egg on top of rice is absolute heaven.
alt: Rice with a sunny-side-up egg is the ultimate comfort food.
note: fried egg = 계란 프라이 / heaven = 최고 / comfort food = 소울 푸드

korean: 무지개 떴다! 진짜 예쁘다 사진 찍어야지
english: There's a rainbow! It's gorgeous. I need to take a picture.
alt: Look a rainbow! So beautiful. Quick let me snap a photo.
note: rainbow = 무지개 / gorgeous = 아름다운

korean: 주말 농장 체험 가볼까? 아이들도 좋아할 것 같아
english: How about a farm experience this weekend? The kids would love it.
alt: Let's visit a farm this weekend. It'd be great for the children.
note: farm experience = 농장 체험

korean: 넌 혼자가 아니야 항상 기억해
english: You're not alone. Always remember that.
alt: Never forget that you have people who care about you.
note: you're not alone = 혼자가 아니야 / remember = 기억해

korean: 우리 같이 늙어가자
english: Let's grow old together.
alt: I want to spend the rest of my life with you.
note: grow old together = 같이 늙어가다

korean: 방 정리 좀 해라 엉망진창이야
english: Clean up your room. It's a total mess.
alt: Your room is a disaster zone. Tidy it up.
note: clean up / tidy up = 정리하다 / disaster zone = 엉망인 곳

korean: 야 너 때문에 항상 힘이 나
english: You always give me strength.
alt: I always feel energized because of you.
note: strength = 힘 / energized = 에너지가 생기는

korean: 이거 한정판인가요? 몇 개 남았어요?
english: Is this a limited edition? How many are left?
alt: Is this a special release? Are there many in stock?
note: limited edition = 한정판 / in stock = 재고 있는

korean: 오늘 하루도 수고했어 푹 쉬어
english: You worked hard today. Get plenty of rest.
alt: Another day done. Rest up tonight.
note: rest up = 푹 쉬다 / worked hard = 수고한

korean: 조카 돌잔치에 가야 해 선물 뭐가 좋을까?
english: I need to go to my niece/nephew's first birthday party. What gift would be good?
alt: I've got a first birthday party to attend. Any gift ideas?
note: first birthday party = 돌잔치 / gift = 선물

korean: 이따 6시에 역에서 만나자
english: Let's meet at the station at 6 later.
alt: See you at 6 by the station.
note: station = 역 / later = 이따

korean: 댓글 창 분위기 무섭다
english: The comment section is scary.
alt: The comments are brutal.
note: comment section = 댓글 창 / brutal = 무서운

korean: 손이 트고 갈라져서 핸드크림 바르고 다녀
english: My hands are chapped and cracking. I carry hand cream everywhere.
alt: I've got dry cracked hands. Hand cream is a must.
note: chapped = 튼 / cracking = 갈라지는 / hand cream = 핸드크림

korean: 아 진짜 후회돼 그때 왜 그랬을까
english: I really regret it. Why did I do that back then?
alt: I'm kicking myself over that decision.
note: regret = 후회 / kicking myself = 자책하는

korean: 여기 음식이 양이 많아서 좋아
english: I love how the portions here are generous.
alt: The servings at this place are huge. Such great value.
note: portions / servings = 양 / generous / huge = 넉넉한

korean: 오늘 체감온도가 영하 15도래 완전 꽁꽁 얼겠다
english: The windchill is minus 15 today. We're going to freeze.
alt: It'll feel like negative 15 with the windchill. Bundle up.
note: windchill = 체감온도 / freeze = 얼다 / bundle up = 따뜻하게 입다

korean: 요즘 클라이밍 다녀 전신 운동 되더라
english: I've been going climbing lately. It's a great full-body workout.
alt: I started bouldering and it works out your whole body.
note: climbing / bouldering = 클라이밍 / full-body workout = 전신 운동

korean: 네가 어떤 선택을 하든 응원할게
english: I'll support you no matter what you decide.
alt: Whatever choice you make I've got your back.
note: support = 응원하다 / I've got your back = 내가 뒤에 있어

korean: 우리 아직 서로 좋아하는 거 맞지?
english: We still like each other right?
alt: We're still into each other aren't we?
note: still like each other = 아직 서로 좋아하는 / into each other = 서로에게 빠진

korean: 문 앞에 택배 놓여있어 가져와
english: There's a package at the front door. Bring it in.
alt: A delivery was left outside the door. Can you get it?
note: package / delivery = 택배 / bring it in = 가져오다

korean: 야 너 진짜 요리 실력 늘었다
english: Your cooking skills have really improved.
alt: You've gotten so much better at cooking.
note: cooking skills = 요리 실력 / improved = 향상된

korean: 이거 AS 가능한가요?
english: Is this covered under warranty?
alt: Can I get after-sales service on this?
note: warranty = 보증 / after-sales service = AS

korean: 야 잘 자 내일 보자!
english: Night! See you tomorrow!
alt: Sleep well! Catch you tomorrow!
note: night = 잘 자 / catch you = 보자

korean: 시누이가 오늘 집에 오신대 준비해야 해
english: My sister-in-law is coming over today. I need to get ready.
alt: My husband's sister is visiting. I should prepare.
note: sister-in-law = 시누이 / prepare = 준비하다

korean: 어디서 만날까? 네가 편한 데로 하자
english: Where should we meet? Let's go with wherever is easiest for you.
alt: You pick the spot. I'll go wherever's convenient for you.
note: convenient = 편리한 / you pick = 네가 골라

korean: 이 계정 차단해야겠다 스팸이 너무 많아
english: I need to block this account. There's way too much spam.
alt: I'm going to block this page. The spam is out of control.
note: block = 차단하다 / spam = 스팸

korean: 허리 펴고 앉아 자세 바르게 해
english: Sit up straight. Fix your posture.
alt: Straighten your back. Your posture is terrible.
note: sit up straight = 허리 펴고 앉다 / posture = 자세

korean: 너무 감동이야 평생 잊지 못할 것 같아
english: I'm so touched. I'll remember this forever.
alt: This is so meaningful. I'll never forget this moment.
note: touched = 감동받은 / remember forever = 영원히 기억하다

korean: 집밥이 제일 맛있어 역시 엄마 손맛이야
english: Nothing beats a home-cooked meal. Mom's cooking is the best.
alt: Homemade food is unbeatable. Mom's got the magic touch.
note: home-cooked meal = 집밥 / magic touch = 마법의 손길

korean: 올해 단풍이 유난히 예쁘다
english: The autumn leaves are exceptionally beautiful this year.
alt: The fall foliage is stunning this season.
note: autumn leaves / fall foliage = 단풍 / exceptionally = 유난히

korean: 요즘 피아노 연습 중인데 손가락이 안 따라가
english: I've been practicing piano but my fingers can't keep up.
alt: I'm working on piano but my fingers aren't cooperating.
note: practicing piano = 피아노 연습 / keep up = 따라가다

korean: 하고 싶은 대로 해 네 선택을 존중해
english: Do what you want. I respect your decision.
alt: Follow your heart. I honor your choice.
note: respect / honor = 존중하다 / follow your heart = 하고 싶은 대로 해

korean: 진짜 좋아하는 사람 앞에서는 말이 안 나와
english: I can't find words in front of someone I really like.
alt: I get completely tongue-tied around my crush.
note: tongue-tied = 말이 안 나오는 / crush = 짝사랑

korean: 욕실 거울 닦아야겠다 물때가 심해
english: I need to clean the bathroom mirror. The water stains are bad.
alt: The mirror has so much limescale. It needs a good scrub.
note: water stains / limescale = 물때 / scrub = 닦다

korean: 야 너 진짜 숨은 실력자다
english: You're a hidden gem.
alt: I had no idea you were this talented.
note: hidden gem = 숨은 보석 / talented = 재능 있는

korean: 할부로 할 수 있나요?
english: Can I pay in installments?
alt: Is financing available for this?
note: installments = 할부 / financing = 금융 결제

korean: 몸조심해 환절기에 감기 걸리기 쉬워
english: Take care of yourself. It's easy to catch a cold during seasonal changes.
alt: Look after yourself. This time of year is rough for getting sick.
note: take care / look after yourself = 몸조심해

korean: 아들이 첫 월급 받았대 기특하다
english: My son got his first paycheck. I'm so proud.
alt: Our boy earned his first salary. How touching.
note: first paycheck / first salary = 첫 월급 / proud = 자랑스러운

korean: 이번 주 금요일 퇴근 후에 볼 수 있어?
english: Can we meet after work this Friday?
alt: Are you free Friday evening after you get off?
note: after work / after you get off = 퇴근 후

korean: 이 영상 조회수 100만 넘었어 대박이다
english: This video hit over a million views. That's insane.
alt: My video just crossed 1M views. Unbelievable.
note: million views = 100만 조회수 / hit / crossed = 넘었다

korean: 요즘 어깨가 오십견인 것 같아
english: I think I might have frozen shoulder.
alt: My shoulder's been acting up. Could be frozen shoulder.
note: frozen shoulder = 오십견 / acting up = 문제를 일으키는

korean: 자존감이 많이 떨어졌어 나 자신이 한심해
english: My self-esteem has really dropped. I feel pathetic.
alt: I'm feeling really down on myself. My confidence is shot.
note: self-esteem = 자존감 / pathetic = 한심한 / confidence is shot = 자신감이 바닥

korean: 곱창 먹어본 적 있어? 완전 맛있어
english: Have you ever tried gopchang? It's so delicious.
alt: Ever had intestine BBQ? It's an absolute must-try.
note: gopchang = 곱창 / must-try = 꼭 먹어봐야 할

korean: 아침저녁으로 일교차가 크니까 겹쳐 입어
english: The temperature difference between morning and evening is big so layer up.
alt: The day-to-night temp gap is huge. Dress in layers.
note: temperature difference = 일교차 / layer up / dress in layers = 겹쳐 입다

korean: 수영 배우기 시작했는데 물이 무서워
english: I started learning to swim but I'm scared of the water.
alt: I'm taking swimming lessons but I have a fear of water.
note: swimming lessons = 수영 레슨 / fear of water = 물 공포

korean: 어떤 결과가 나오든 이미 넌 대단해
english: Whatever the result you're already amazing.
alt: Regardless of the outcome you should be proud of yourself.
note: regardless of = ~와 상관없이 / proud = 자랑스러운

korean: 너한테 반했어 첫눈에
english: I fell for you at first sight.
alt: It was love at first sight for me.
note: fell for = 반하다 / love at first sight = 첫눈에 사랑

korean: 베란다 빨래 걷어야 하는데 비 올 것 같아
english: I need to bring in the laundry from the veranda. It looks like rain.
alt: Better grab the clothes off the balcony before it rains.
note: bring in = 걷다 / veranda / balcony = 베란다

korean: 야 우리 맨날 이렇게 놀자 진짜 재밌다
english: Let's hang out like this all the time. This is so fun.
alt: I wish we could do this every day. Best time ever.
note: hang out = 놀다 / best time ever = 최고로 즐거운 시간

korean: 이거 마지막 하나인가요?
english: Is this the last one?
alt: Are there any more or is this it?
note: the last one = 마지막 하나

korean: 어디 아프지 말고 건강해
english: Don't get sick. Stay healthy.
alt: I hope you always stay in good health.
note: stay healthy = 건강해 / in good health = 건강한 상태로

korean: 조부모님이 시골에서 올라오셨어 반가워
english: My grandparents came up from the countryside. So happy to see them.
alt: Grandma and grandpa made the trip from the country. What a nice surprise.
note: grandparents = 조부모 / countryside = 시골

korean: 다음 주 수요일로 미루자 이번 주는 좀 빠듯해
english: Let's push it to next Wednesday. This week is a bit tight.
alt: Can we postpone to next Wednesday? I'm swamped this week.
note: push / postpone = 미루다 / swamped = 매우 바쁜

korean: 계정 비공개로 바꿀까 고민 중이야
english: I'm thinking about switching my account to private.
alt: I'm considering going private on my account.
note: private = 비공개 / switch / going = 바꾸다

korean: 발바닥이 아파서 인솔 깔아야겠다
english: My feet are hurting. I need to get some insoles.
alt: The soles of my feet are sore. I should invest in some insoles.
note: insoles = 인솔 / soles of feet = 발바닥

korean: 진짜 존경스러워 어떻게 그렇게 할 수 있어?
english: I really admire you. How can you do that?
alt: That's truly admirable. How do you manage it?
note: admire / admirable = 존경하는 / 존경스러운

korean: 이 카페 빵이 갓 구운 거라 진짜 맛있어
english: The bread at this café is freshly baked and so delicious.
alt: They bake their pastries fresh here and you can totally tell.
note: freshly baked = 갓 구운 / pastries = 빵류

korean: 요즘 밤이 길어졌다 해가 빨리 진다
english: The nights are getting longer. The sun sets so early.
alt: Days are getting shorter. It gets dark so fast now.
note: nights getting longer = 밤이 길어지는 / sun sets = 해가 지다

korean: 요즘 러닝 하는데 3킬로까지 뛸 수 있어
english: I've been running and I can go up to 3K now.
alt: I started jogging and I've worked my way up to 3 kilometers.
note: running / jogging = 러닝 / worked my way up = 점점 늘려가다

korean: 내가 대신할 수 있는 거면 대신해줄게
english: If there's anything I can do for you I will.
alt: I'd take your place if I could.
note: take your place = 대신하다

korean: 보고 싶어 죽겠어 빨리 만나자
english: I miss you so much it hurts. Let's meet up soon.
alt: I'm dying to see you. When can we meet?
note: miss you so much = 보고 싶어 죽겠다 / dying to = ~하고 싶어 죽겠다

korean: 리모컨 어디 갔지? 또 쿠션 밑이겠지
english: Where did the remote go? Probably under the cushion again.
alt: I lost the remote again. I bet it's in the couch.
note: remote = 리모컨 / cushion = 쿠션 / couch = 소파

korean: 야 진짜 웃기다 배꼽 빠지겠어
english: That's absolutely hilarious. I'm dying.
alt: I can't breathe from laughing. That's too much.
note: hilarious = 매우 웃긴 / dying = 죽겠다 / can't breathe = 숨을 못 쉬겠다

korean: 영수증 가져오면 환불해드릴게요
english: If you bring the receipt we can process a refund.
alt: Just bring your receipt and we'll give you a full refund.
note: receipt = 영수증 / process a refund = 환불 처리하다

korean: 연락할게 또 만나자!
english: I'll be in touch. Let's meet again!
alt: I'll hit you up. Until next time!
note: be in touch = 연락하다 / hit you up = 연락하다 (캐주얼)

korean: 삼촌이 용돈 주셔서 기분이 좋았어
english: My uncle gave me some pocket money and it made my day.
alt: Uncle slipped me some cash. What a treat.
note: pocket money = 용돈 / made my day = 기분이 좋았다

korean: 오늘 약속 잊지 않았지? 리마인드 차
english: You didn't forget about today's plans right? Just a reminder.
alt: Friendly reminder about our plans today. Still on?
note: reminder = 리마인드 / still on = 아직 유효하지?

korean: 유튜브 알림 설정 해놨어 새 영상 올라오면 바로 볼 거야
english: I turned on YouTube notifications. I'll watch as soon as a new video drops.
alt: I've got notifications on. I'll catch every new upload immediately.
note: notifications = 알림 / drops = 올라오다 / upload = 업로드

korean: 물 많이 마셔 하루에 2리터는 마셔야 해
english: Drink lots of water. You should have at least 2 liters a day.
alt: Stay hydrated. Aim for 2 liters daily.
note: stay hydrated = 수분을 유지하다 / aim for = 목표로 하다

korean: 뭔가 허전해 공허한 느낌이야
english: Something feels missing. I feel empty inside.
alt: I've got this hollow feeling and I can't shake it.
note: empty / hollow = 공허한 / shake it = 떨쳐내다

korean: 김치볶음밥에 치즈 올려주세요
english: Can I get cheese on my kimchi fried rice?
alt: Put some cheese on the kimchi bokkeumbap please.
note: kimchi fried rice = 김치볶음밥 / cheese = 치즈

korean: 날씨가 화창해서 빨래 널기 딱 좋다
english: The weather is so sunny it's perfect for hanging laundry.
alt: What a bright day. Great drying weather for the wash.
note: sunny / bright = 화창한 / hanging laundry = 빨래 널기

korean: 요즘 수채화 그리는 게 재밌어
english: I've been enjoying watercolor painting lately.
alt: Watercolors have become my new favorite hobby.
note: watercolor = 수채화 / favorite hobby = 최애 취미

korean: 아무리 어두워도 별은 빛나는 법이야
english: No matter how dark it gets the stars still shine.
alt: Even in the darkest night the stars find a way to glow.
note: stars shine = 별은 빛나다 / even in the darkest night = 가장 어두운 밤에도

korean: 오래오래 행복하자 우리
english: Let's be happy together for a long long time.
alt: Here's to a lifetime of happiness together.
note: a lifetime of happiness = 평생의 행복

korean: 김치냉장고 정리해야 하는데 양이 장난 아니야
english: I need to organize the kimchi fridge but there's so much in there.
alt: The kimchi refrigerator is overflowing. It needs a serious sort-out.
note: kimchi fridge = 김치냉장고 / overflowing = 넘치는

korean: 너 덕분에 매일이 즐거워 진심이야
english: Every day is fun because of you. I mean it.
alt: You genuinely make my days brighter. From the bottom of my heart.
note: mean it = 진심이야 / from the bottom of my heart = 진심으로

korean: 이거 오늘만 세일인가요?
english: Is this sale for today only?
alt: Is this a one-day-only deal?
note: today only / one-day-only = 오늘만

korean: 편안한 밤 되세요 내일 뵙겠습니다
english: Have a restful evening. See you tomorrow.
alt: Good evening. I look forward to seeing you tomorrow.
note: restful = 편안한 / look forward to = ~를 기대하다

korean: 택배 언제 오는지 추적해봐야겠다
english: I should track when the delivery is arriving.
alt: Let me check the tracking on that package.
note: track = 추적하다 / delivery = 택배

korean: 잠깐만 화장실 좀 다녀올게
english: Hold on. I need to use the restroom.
alt: Give me a sec. Nature calls.
note: restroom = 화장실 / nature calls = 화장실 가야 해 (유머)

korean: 요즘 스트레스 받아서 단 거 당긴다
english: I've been stressed so I'm craving sweets.
alt: Stress eating is real. I need something sugary.
note: craving sweets = 단 것이 당기는 / stress eating = 스트레스 폭식

korean: 우리 강아지가 자꾸 짖어서 이웃한테 미안해
english: My dog keeps barking and I feel bad for the neighbors.
alt: Our pup won't stop barking. I'm worried about complaints.
note: bark = 짖다 / feel bad = 미안하다

korean: 산책 갈 시간이야 목줄 어디 뒀지?
english: It's time for a walk. Where did I put the leash?
alt: Walk time! Have you seen the leash anywhere?
note: leash = 목줄 / walk = 산책

korean: 고양이가 키보드 위에 올라와서 일을 못 하겠어
english: My cat keeps sitting on my keyboard so I can't work.
alt: The cat has claimed my keyboard. No work for me today.
note: claimed = 차지하다 / keyboard = 키보드

korean: 동물병원 예약 잡아야 하는데 백신 맞을 때 됐어
english: I need to book a vet appointment. It's time for vaccinations.
alt: Gotta schedule a vet visit. Shots are due.
note: vet = 수의사 / vaccination = 백신 접종

korean: 우리 집 고양이 까칠해서 손님 오면 숨어
english: My cat is so grumpy it hides whenever guests come over.
alt: Our cat's a real diva. It vanishes when company arrives.
note: grumpy = 까칠한 / vanishes = 사라지다

korean: 강아지 털이 너무 빠져서 청소가 끝이 없어
english: My dog sheds so much the cleaning never ends.
alt: The dog hair is everywhere. I vacuum three times a day.
note: shed = 털이 빠지다 / vacuum = 청소기 돌리다

korean: 간식 줄 때 앉아 기다리라고 훈련 중이야
english: I'm training him to sit and wait before giving treats.
alt: We're working on the sit-and-wait command at treat time.
note: training = 훈련 / sit = 앉아 / treat = 간식

korean: 유기견 입양하고 싶은데 고민이야
english: I'm thinking about adopting a rescue dog but I'm on the fence.
alt: I want to adopt a shelter dog but I'm still deciding.
note: rescue dog = 유기견 / on the fence = 고민 중인

korean: 고양이 화장실 모래 갈아줘야 해
english: I need to change the cat litter.
alt: The litter box needs a fresh scoop.
note: cat litter = 고양이 모래 / litter box = 화장실

korean: 강아지가 신발을 물어뜯어서 새로 사야 해
english: My dog chewed up my shoes, so I need to buy new ones.
alt: The puppy destroyed my shoes again. Time for a new pair.
note: chewed up = 물어뜯다 / destroyed = 망가뜨리다

korean: 우리 강아지 미용 시키러 가야 해
english: I need to take my dog to the groomer.
alt: Time to get the pup a haircut.
note: groomer = 애완동물 미용사 / haircut = 미용

korean: 고양이가 박스만 보면 들어가
english: My cat jumps into any box it sees.
alt: Cats and boxes. Mine can't resist any of them.
note: can't resist = 저항 못 하다 / box = 박스

korean: 펫보험 들어야 하나 병원비가 장난 아니야
english: Should I get pet insurance? Vet bills are no joke.
alt: Pet insurance might be worth it. Those vet bills add up fast.
note: pet insurance = 펫보험 / vet bills = 병원비

korean: 강아지가 분리불안이 있어서 혼자 두면 울어
english: My dog has separation anxiety and cries when left alone.
alt: The pup gets anxious whenever I leave. He whines the whole time.
note: separation anxiety = 분리불안 / whines = 낑낑거리다

korean: 고양이 캣타워 새로 사줬더니 박스에서만 놀아
english: I bought a new cat tower but the cat only plays in the box it came in.
alt: Spent a fortune on a cat tree and she prefers the cardboard box.
note: cat tower = 캣타워 / cardboard box = 박스

korean: 산책하다가 다른 강아지 만나면 너무 흥분해
english: My dog gets so excited when it meets other dogs on walks.
alt: He goes absolutely crazy when he spots another dog on our walk.
note: excited = 흥분한 / spots = 발견하다

korean: 오늘 헬스장 가야 하는데 너무 귀찮아
english: I should go to the gym today but I really don't feel like it.
alt: Gym day today but I'm so not in the mood.
note: gym = 헬스장 / not in the mood = 기분이 안 나는

korean: 러닝 시작했는데 무릎이 좀 아파
english: I started running but my knees are bothering me.
alt: I picked up running and now my knees are acting up.
note: bothering me = 아프다 / acting up = 문제가 생기다

korean: 오늘 스쿼트 100개 했더니 다리가 후들후들해
english: I did 100 squats today and my legs are shaking like crazy.
alt: After 100 squats my legs feel like jelly.
note: squats = 스쿼트 / like jelly = 후들후들한

korean: 요가 시작했는데 유연성이 진짜 바닥이야
english: I started yoga but my flexibility is absolutely terrible.
alt: I tried yoga and realized I'm as stiff as a board.
note: flexibility = 유연성 / stiff as a board = 뻣뻣한

korean: 수영장 등록했어 이번 달부터 다닐 거야
english: I signed up for a swimming pool membership starting this month.
alt: I enrolled at the pool. Starting my swim routine this month.
note: signed up = 등록하다 / membership = 회원권

korean: PT 받고 있는데 트레이너가 너무 빡세
english: I'm doing personal training and my trainer is super intense.
alt: My PT sessions are brutal. The trainer pushes me to the limit.
note: personal training = PT / intense = 빡센

korean: 운동 끝나고 프로틴 셰이크 마셔야 해
english: After working out, I need to have a protein shake.
alt: Time for a post-workout protein shake.
note: protein shake = 프로틴 셰이크 / post-workout = 운동 후

korean: 등산 같이 갈 사람 구해
english: I'm looking for someone to go hiking with.
alt: Anyone down for a hike? Need a hiking buddy.
note: hiking = 등산 / hiking buddy = 등산 동반자

korean: 플랭크 1분도 못 버티겠어
english: I can't even hold a plank for one minute.
alt: A one-minute plank feels like an eternity to me.
note: plank = 플랭크 / hold = 버티다

korean: 3대 운동 합이 300 넘었어
english: My big three total is over 300 now.
alt: I finally hit a 300-plus total on bench squat and deadlift.
note: big three = 3대 운동 (벤치 스쿼트 데드리프트)

korean: 운동 쉬었더니 다시 시작하기 힘들다
english: Taking a break from working out makes it so hard to start again.
alt: Getting back into fitness after a break is the worst.
note: break = 쉬는 기간 / getting back into = 다시 시작하다

korean: 홈트 영상 따라 하고 있어
english: I'm following along with home workout videos.
alt: I've been doing home workouts following YouTube tutorials.
note: home workout = 홈트 / following along = 따라 하다

korean: 근육통이 심해서 계단도 못 내려가겠어
english: My muscles are so sore I can barely walk down the stairs.
alt: The DOMS hit hard. Stairs are my enemy right now.
note: sore = 아픈 / DOMS = 지연성 근육통

korean: 스트레칭 안 하면 다칠 수 있어
english: You could get injured if you skip stretching.
alt: Don't skip your stretches or you'll pull something.
note: stretching = 스트레칭 / pull something = 근육을 다치다

korean: 머리 잘랐는데 어때? 괜찮아?
english: I got a haircut. What do you think? Does it look okay?
alt: Just got my hair done. How does it look?
note: haircut = 머리 자르기 / how does it look = 어때 보여?

korean: 파마하려고 하는데 어떤 스타일이 어울릴까?
english: I'm thinking of getting a perm. What style would suit me?
alt: I want a perm. Any suggestions on what would look good on me?
note: perm = 파마 / suit = 어울리다

korean: 앞머리 좀 다듬어 주세요 눈에 걸려요
english: Can you trim my bangs? They're getting in my eyes.
alt: Just a bang trim please. They're too long.
note: trim = 다듬다 / bangs = 앞머리

korean: 염색 하고 싶은데 머리 상할까 봐 걱정이야
english: I want to dye my hair but I'm worried about damage.
alt: I'd love to color my hair but I'm nervous about the damage.
note: dye = 염색하다 / damage = 손상

korean: 피부가 건조해서 수분크림 바꿔야 할 것 같아
english: My skin is so dry I think I need to switch moisturizers.
alt: I need a better moisturizer. My skin is super parched.
note: moisturizer = 수분크림 / parched = 매우 건조한

korean: 네일 받으러 갈 건데 같이 갈래?
english: I'm going to get my nails done. Want to come with?
alt: Heading to the nail salon. Wanna join?
note: nails done = 네일 받다 / nail salon = 네일샵

korean: 탈색하니까 머리가 엄청 빠져
english: My hair is falling out so much after bleaching.
alt: Bleaching really did a number on my hair. It's shedding like crazy.
note: bleaching = 탈색 / shedding = 빠지는

korean: 피부과 가서 여드름 치료 받아야겠다
english: I should go to a dermatologist for acne treatment.
alt: Time to see a dermatologist about this breakout.
note: dermatologist = 피부과 / acne = 여드름 / breakout = 피부 트러블

korean: 요즘 다크서클이 심해서 컨실러 없으면 못 나가
english: My dark circles are so bad I can't leave the house without concealer.
alt: The bags under my eyes are terrible. Concealer is a must.
note: dark circles = 다크서클 / concealer = 컨실러

korean: 미용실 예약 잡았어 토요일 2시
english: I booked a salon appointment for Saturday at 2.
alt: Got a hair appointment at 2 on Saturday.
note: salon appointment = 미용실 예약

korean: 선크림 안 바르면 안 돼 자외선 완전 강해
english: You have to put on sunscreen. The UV rays are super strong.
alt: Don't skip sunscreen. The sun is brutal today.
note: sunscreen = 선크림 / UV rays = 자외선

korean: 지하철 환승 어디서 해야 해?
english: Where do I transfer on the subway?
alt: Which station do I need to change trains at?
note: transfer = 환승하다 / change trains = 갈아타다

korean: 버스 방금 놓쳤어 다음 거 언제 와?
english: I just missed the bus. When's the next one?
alt: The bus just left without me. How long till the next?
note: missed = 놓치다 / how long till = 언제까지

korean: 출근 시간에 지하철 진짜 미어터져
english: The subway is packed during rush hour. It's insane.
alt: The morning commute train is absolutely sardine-can level.
note: packed = 꽉 찬 / rush hour = 출근 시간 / sardine-can = 콩나물 시루

korean: 택시 잡기 힘들어 앱으로 불러야겠다
english: It's hard to hail a cab. I'll call one through an app.
alt: Can't find a taxi anywhere. Let me book one on the app.
note: hail a cab = 택시 잡다 / book = 예약하다

korean: 내비 켜봐 길 막힐 수도 있어
english: Turn on the GPS. There might be traffic.
alt: Fire up the navigation. We might hit congestion.
note: GPS = 내비 / traffic = 교통 체증 / congestion = 혼잡

korean: 고속도로 정체 심하다 우회할까?
english: The highway is backed up. Should we take a detour?
alt: Traffic on the freeway is terrible. Want to try an alternate route?
note: backed up = 정체된 / detour = 우회로

korean: 주차장 만차야 다른 데 찾아봐야 해
english: The parking lot is full. We need to find another one.
alt: No spots left. Let's look for parking somewhere else.
note: parking lot = 주차장 / full = 만차

korean: 신호 위반하면 벌금 나와 조심해
english: You'll get fined if you run a red light. Be careful.
alt: Watch it. Running a light will cost you a ticket.
note: fined = 벌금 / run a red light = 신호 위반

korean: 여기서 몇 번 출구로 나가야 해?
english: Which exit should I take from here?
alt: What exit number do I need?
note: exit = 출구

korean: 대리운전 불러야겠다 술 마셨으니까
english: I should call a designated driver since I've been drinking.
alt: Better get a driver service. I've had a few drinks.
note: designated driver = 대리운전

korean: 킥보드 타고 갈까? 가까우니까
english: Should we take a scooter? It's close by.
alt: Let's hop on an e-scooter. It's not far.
note: scooter = 킥보드 / close by = 가까운

korean: 자전거 타고 출근하니까 건강해진 느낌이야
english: Commuting by bike makes me feel healthier.
alt: I've been cycling to work and I feel so much better.
note: commuting = 출퇴근 / cycling = 자전거 타기

korean: 여보세요 지금 통화 가능하세요?
english: Hello? Are you available to talk right now?
alt: Hi there. Is this a good time to chat?
note: available = 가능한 / good time = 좋은 시간

korean: 전화 잠깐만요 시끄러워서 안 들려요
english: Hold on a second. It's too loud and I can't hear you.
alt: Can you hang on? I need to move somewhere quieter.
note: hold on = 잠깐만 / loud = 시끄러운

korean: 나중에 다시 전화할게 지금 좀 바빠
english: I'll call you back later. I'm a bit busy right now.
alt: Let me ring you back. I'm tied up at the moment.
note: call back = 다시 전화하다 / tied up = 바쁜

korean: 문자로 보내줄래? 전화 못 받을 것 같아
english: Can you text me instead? I probably won't be able to answer.
alt: Just shoot me a text. I might miss your call.
note: text = 문자 / shoot me a text = 문자 보내줘

korean: 전화 끊지 마 잠깐만 확인할게
english: Don't hang up. Let me check something real quick.
alt: Stay on the line. I just need to look something up.
note: hang up = 전화 끊다 / stay on the line = 끊지 마

korean: 수신자 부담 전화인가요?
english: Is this a collect call?
alt: Am I being charged for this call?
note: collect call = 수신자 부담 전화

korean: 통화 품질이 안 좋아 끊겼다 연결됐다 해
english: The call quality is terrible. It keeps cutting in and out.
alt: The connection is awful. You're breaking up.
note: call quality = 통화 품질 / breaking up = 끊기는

korean: 스팸 전화 진짜 많다 하루에 몇 번씩 와
english: I get so many spam calls. Several times a day.
alt: The robocalls are out of control. They ring nonstop.
note: spam calls = 스팸 전화 / robocalls = 자동 전화

korean: 영상통화 할까? 얼굴 보고 싶어
english: Want to video call? I want to see your face.
alt: Let's FaceTime. I miss seeing you.
note: video call = 영상통화 / FaceTime = 화상통화

korean: 부재중 전화가 3통이나 있네 누구지?
english: I have 3 missed calls. Who could it be?
alt: Three missed calls. Wonder who's trying to reach me.
note: missed calls = 부재중 전화

korean: 택배 언제 도착해? 배송 조회해봤어?
english: When will the delivery arrive? Did you check the tracking?
alt: Any update on the package? Have you tracked it?
note: delivery = 택배 / tracking = 배송 조회

korean: 배달 시켜 먹자 나가기 귀찮아
english: Let's order delivery. I don't feel like going out.
alt: Let's just get food delivered. Too lazy to go anywhere.
note: order delivery = 배달 시키다 / lazy = 귀찮은

korean: 택배 왔는데 부재중이라 경비실에 맡겨졌대
english: The package came but I wasn't home so it was left at the security desk.
alt: Missed the delivery. They dropped it off at the front desk.
note: security desk = 경비실 / dropped off = 맡기다

korean: 배달비가 너무 비싸 직접 포장해올까?
english: The delivery fee is too expensive. Should I just pick it up?
alt: Delivery charges are insane. Maybe we should do takeout.
note: delivery fee = 배달비 / pick it up = 포장해오다

korean: 리뷰 보고 시켰는데 사진이랑 다르잖아
english: I ordered based on reviews but it looks nothing like the photos.
alt: The reviews were misleading. This is not what I expected.
note: reviews = 리뷰 / looks nothing like = 전혀 다르다

korean: 택배 박스가 쌓여서 분리수거 해야 해
english: Delivery boxes are piling up. I need to take out the recycling.
alt: The cardboard boxes are stacking up. Time for a recycling run.
note: piling up = 쌓이다 / recycling = 분리수거

korean: 배달 예상 시간이 1시간이래 너무 오래 걸린다
english: The estimated delivery time is an hour. That's way too long.
alt: An hour wait for delivery? That's ridiculous.
note: estimated time = 예상 시간

korean: 오배송됐어 다른 사람 거 왔어
english: They delivered the wrong package. I got someone else's order.
alt: Wrong delivery. This isn't what I ordered.
note: wrong package = 오배송

korean: 편의점 택배로 보내면 더 싸
english: It's cheaper to send it via convenience store delivery.
alt: Shipping through a convenience store saves money.
note: convenience store = 편의점 / saves money = 돈 아끼다

korean: 쿠팡 로켓배송이라 내일 오겠다
english: It's Coupang Rocket Delivery so it should arrive tomorrow.
alt: Ordered on Coupang with express shipping. Should be here by tomorrow.
note: Rocket Delivery = 로켓배송 / express shipping = 빠른 배송

korean: 계좌이체 해줄게 계좌번호 알려줘
english: I'll wire you the money. What's your account number?
alt: Let me transfer it to you. Send me your account details.
note: wire = 송금하다 / account number = 계좌번호

korean: 적금 만기 됐는데 연장할까 해지할까
english: My savings account matured. Should I renew or close it?
alt: The fixed deposit is up. Debating whether to roll it over or cash out.
note: matured = 만기 / renew = 연장 / cash out = 해지

korean: 대출 이자율이 올라서 부담이 커
english: Loan interest rates went up and the burden is getting heavier.
alt: The rate hike is killing me. My loan payments are brutal.
note: interest rate = 이자율 / rate hike = 금리 인상

korean: 카드값 얼마 나왔어? 이번 달 많이 썼나?
english: How much is my credit card bill? Did I spend a lot this month?
alt: What's the damage on my card this month?
note: credit card bill = 카드값 / what's the damage = 얼마나 나왔어

korean: ATM에서 현금 뽑아야 해 잔액 부족이래
english: I need to withdraw cash from the ATM. It says insufficient funds.
alt: The ATM says I'm low on funds. Gotta check my balance.
note: withdraw = 인출 / insufficient funds = 잔액 부족

korean: 주식 좀 올랐다 팔까 말까 고민이야
english: My stocks went up a bit. I'm debating whether to sell.
alt: Stocks are up. Should I cash in or hold?
note: stocks = 주식 / cash in = 매도하다 / hold = 보유하다

korean: 월세 이체 깜빡했어 오늘 보내야 해
english: I forgot to transfer the rent. I need to send it today.
alt: Totally forgot rent was due. Gotta wire it ASAP.
note: rent = 월세 / due = 만기인

korean: 체크카드 분실 신고해야 해 어디서 잃어버렸지
english: I need to report my debit card lost. Where did I lose it?
alt: My debit card is missing. I need to cancel it right away.
note: debit card = 체크카드 / report lost = 분실 신고

korean: 연말정산 때 환급 받을 수 있을까?
english: Can I get a tax refund at year-end?
alt: Think I'll get anything back on my tax return?
note: tax refund = 환급 / tax return = 연말정산

korean: 자동이체 설정해놨으니까 신경 안 써도 돼
english: I set up auto-pay so you don't have to worry about it.
alt: Autopay is on. It'll take care of itself.
note: auto-pay = 자동이체

korean: 진료 예약 잡으려고 하는데 이번 주 가능한가요?
english: I'd like to make an appointment. Do you have any openings this week?
alt: Can I schedule a visit for sometime this week?
note: appointment = 예약 / openings = 빈 시간

korean: 아파서 병원 갔더니 감기래 약 처방받았어
english: I went to the doctor because I felt sick, and it turned out to be a cold. I got a prescription.
alt: Went to the clinic. Just a cold. Got some meds.
note: prescription = 처방전 / meds = 약

korean: 혈액검사 결과 나왔어? 수치가 어때?
english: Did the blood test results come back? How are the numbers?
alt: Any news on my blood work? Are the levels normal?
note: blood test = 혈액검사 / levels = 수치

korean: MRI 찍어야 한대 무서워
english: They said I need an MRI. I'm scared.
alt: The doctor wants me to get an MRI and I'm nervous.
note: MRI = 자기공명영상

korean: 약 먹고 나서 졸려서 운전하면 안 돼
english: After taking the medicine I get drowsy, so I shouldn't drive.
alt: This medication makes me sleepy. No driving for me.
note: drowsy = 졸린 / medication = 약

korean: 건강검진 받아야 하는데 계속 미루고 있어
english: I should get a health checkup but I keep putting it off.
alt: I've been procrastinating on my annual physical.
note: health checkup = 건강검진 / putting it off = 미루다

korean: 주사 맞을 때 눈 감아야 안 무서워
english: I close my eyes when getting a shot so it's less scary.
alt: If you don't look at the needle it doesn't hurt as much.
note: shot = 주사 / needle = 바늘

korean: 입원해야 한대 며칠 정도 걸린대
english: They say I need to be hospitalized. It'll take a few days.
alt: I have to be admitted for a couple of days.
note: hospitalized = 입원 / admitted = 입원하다

korean: 대기 시간이 너무 길어 2시간째야
english: The wait time is way too long. It's been 2 hours.
alt: I've been sitting in the waiting room for two hours now.
note: wait time = 대기 시간 / waiting room = 대기실

korean: 보험 청구하려면 서류 뭐 필요해?
english: What documents do I need to file an insurance claim?
alt: What paperwork is required for the insurance claim?
note: insurance claim = 보험 청구 / documents = 서류

korean: 아메리카노 한 잔이요 아이스로요
english: One iced Americano please.
alt: Can I get an iced Americano?
note: iced Americano = 아이스 아메리카노

korean: 여기 와이파이 비밀번호 뭐예요?
english: What's the Wi-Fi password here?
alt: Do you have Wi-Fi? Can I get the password?
note: Wi-Fi password = 와이파이 비밀번호

korean: 카페 자리 잡아놨어 창가 자리야
english: I saved us a spot at the cafe. It's by the window.
alt: Got us a window seat at the cafe.
note: saved a spot = 자리 잡다 / window seat = 창가 자리

korean: 텀블러 가져가면 할인해주나요?
english: Do you get a discount if you bring a tumbler?
alt: Is there a tumbler discount?
note: tumbler = 텀블러 / discount = 할인

korean: 디카페인으로 해주세요 밤에 잠 못 자서
english: Decaf please. I can't sleep at night otherwise.
alt: Make it decaf. Caffeine keeps me up.
note: decaf = 디카페인 / keeps me up = 잠 못 자게 하다

korean: 샷 추가해주세요 오늘 너무 피곤해
english: Can you add an extra shot? I'm so tired today.
alt: Extra shot please. Need the caffeine boost.
note: extra shot = 샷 추가 / caffeine boost = 카페인 충전

korean: 이 카페 분위기 진짜 좋다 인스타에 올려야지
english: The vibe of this cafe is amazing. I need to post it on Instagram.
alt: This place has such a great atmosphere. Definitely gram-worthy.
note: vibe = 분위기 / gram-worthy = 인스타 올릴 만한

korean: 케이크 종류 뭐 있어요? 추천해주세요
english: What kinds of cake do you have? Any recommendations?
alt: What cakes are available? What do you suggest?
note: cake = 케이크 / recommendations = 추천

korean: 포장해갈게요 컵홀더 하나 주세요
english: I'll take it to go. Can I get a cup holder?
alt: To go please. And a carrier if you have one.
note: to go = 포장 / cup holder = 컵홀더

korean: 카페 멤버십 적립할게요
english: I'd like to earn points on my cafe membership.
alt: Can you stamp my loyalty card?
note: membership = 멤버십 / loyalty card = 적립 카드

korean: 오늘 한잔하자 스트레스 풀어야 해
english: Let's grab a drink tonight. I need to blow off some steam.
alt: Let's hit the bar. I need to unwind.
note: grab a drink = 한잔하다 / blow off steam = 스트레스 풀다

korean: 소맥 비율 어떻게 해? 3대7?
english: What ratio do you do for soju-beer? 3 to 7?
alt: How do you mix your somaek? Three-seven?
note: soju-beer = 소맥 / ratio = 비율

korean: 안주 뭐 시킬까? 치킨 어때?
english: What should we order for snacks? How about chicken?
alt: What anju should we get? I'm thinking chicken.
note: snacks = 안주 / anju = 안주 (Korean drinking food)

korean: 나 오늘 진짜 취했어 택시 타고 갈게
english: I'm really drunk tonight. I'll take a cab home.
alt: I'm way too wasted. Gonna grab a taxi.
note: drunk = 취한 / wasted = 만취한

korean: 건배! 우리의 우정을 위하여!
english: Cheers! To our friendship!
alt: Here's to us! Bottoms up!
note: cheers = 건배 / bottoms up = 원샷

korean: 숙취가 장난 아니야 해장국 먹으러 가자
english: This hangover is brutal. Let's go get some hangover soup.
alt: I'm so hungover. I need some haejangguk ASAP.
note: hangover = 숙취 / hangover soup = 해장국

korean: 한 잔만 더 마시자 벌써 가려고?
english: One more drink. You're leaving already?
alt: Come on one more round. The night is still young.
note: one more drink = 한 잔 더 / the night is young = 아직 이르다

korean: 술 약해서 맥주 한 잔이면 얼굴 빨개져
english: I'm a lightweight. One beer and my face turns red.
alt: I'm a total lightweight. I flush after one drink.
note: lightweight = 술 약한 사람 / flush = 얼굴 빨개지다

korean: 2차 갈까? 노래방 어때?
english: Want to go for round two? How about karaoke?
alt: Shall we hit a noraebang for the after-party?
note: round two = 2차 / karaoke = 노래방

korean: 술자리에서 폭탄주 만들어야지
english: We gotta make bomb shots at the drinking party.
alt: Time for some bomb shots. Let's get this party started.
note: bomb shots = 폭탄주

korean: 옆집에서 너무 시끄러워 밤에 잠을 못 자
english: The neighbors are so loud I can't sleep at night.
alt: Next door is incredibly noisy. I haven't slept properly in days.
note: neighbors = 이웃 / noisy = 시끄러운

korean: 새로 이사 온 이웃에게 인사해야겠다
english: I should introduce myself to the new neighbors.
alt: We should go say hi to the new people who moved in.
note: introduce = 인사하다 / moved in = 이사 온

korean: 층간 소음 때문에 아래층이랑 트러블 있어
english: We're having issues with the downstairs neighbors over floor noise.
alt: There's a dispute with the people below us about noise.
note: floor noise = 층간 소음 / dispute = 분쟁

korean: 이웃집에서 반찬 나눠줬어 진짜 고마워
english: The neighbors shared some side dishes. So kind of them.
alt: Our neighbor dropped off homemade banchan. How sweet!
note: shared = 나눠주다 / banchan = 반찬

korean: 택배 대신 받아주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you receive a package for me?
alt: Would you mind holding a delivery for me while I'm out?
note: receive = 받다 / holding = 보관하다

korean: 벚꽃 피기 시작했어 이번 주말에 꽃구경 가자
english: The cherry blossoms are starting to bloom. Let's go see them this weekend.
alt: Cherry blossom season is here. Weekend picnic under the trees?
note: cherry blossoms = 벚꽃 / bloom = 피다

korean: 여름이라 에어컨 없으면 못 살아
english: It's summer, so I literally can't survive without AC.
alt: AC is non-negotiable in this heat.
note: AC = 에어컨 / non-negotiable = 필수인

korean: 가을 되니까 단풍 구경 가고 싶다
english: Now that it's fall I want to go see the autumn leaves.
alt: Fall foliage season. I'm dying to see the colors.
note: autumn leaves = 단풍 / fall foliage = 가을 단풍

korean: 겨울에 눈 오면 눈사람 만들자
english: If it snows this winter let's build a snowman.
alt: I hope it snows so we can make a snowman.
note: snowman = 눈사람

korean: 장마철이라 우산 꼭 챙겨야 해
english: It's monsoon season so you must bring an umbrella.
alt: Rainy season means always having an umbrella on you.
note: monsoon season = 장마철 / umbrella = 우산

korean: 요즘 일교차가 커서 감기 조심해
english: The temperature difference between day and night is big so be careful not to catch a cold.
alt: Watch out for the weather swings. Easy to get sick.
note: temperature difference = 일교차

korean: 미세먼지 심하다 마스크 써야겠어
english: The fine dust is bad. I should wear a mask.
alt: Air quality is terrible today. Mask up.
note: fine dust = 미세먼지 / air quality = 공기질

korean: 꽃가루 알레르기 때문에 봄이 싫어
english: I hate spring because of pollen allergies.
alt: Spring is my nemesis. Pollen destroys me.
note: pollen allergies = 꽃가루 알레르기

korean: 추석 연휴 어디 가? 고향 내려가?
english: Where are you going for the Chuseok holiday? Heading to your hometown?
alt: Going back home for Chuseok? Got any plans?
note: Chuseok = 추석 / hometown = 고향

korean: 설날에 세뱃돈 얼마 받았어?
english: How much New Year's money did you get?
alt: What did you get in sebae money this Seollal?
note: New Year's money = 세뱃돈 / Seollal = 설날

korean: 명절에 친척들 만나면 질문 폭격이야
english: When I see relatives during holidays the questions are nonstop.
alt: Holidays mean a firing squad of nosy relatives.
note: relatives = 친척 / nonstop = 끊임없는

korean: 송편 같이 만들자 재미있잖아
english: Let's make songpyeon together. It's fun!
alt: Come make rice cakes with me. It'll be a blast!
note: songpyeon = 송편 / a blast = 재미있는

korean: 고속도로 명절 정체 때문에 5시간 걸렸어
english: It took 5 hours because of the holiday traffic jam on the highway.
alt: Holiday highway traffic was insane. Five hours on the road.
note: traffic jam = 정체 / highway = 고속도로

korean: 차례 준비하느라 너무 힘들어
english: Preparing the ancestral rites is exhausting.
alt: Setting up the charye table wipes me out every year.
note: ancestral rites = 차례 / exhausting = 힘든

korean: 생일 축하해! 선물 준비했어
english: Happy birthday! I got you a present.
alt: Happy birthday! I've got a little something for you.
note: happy birthday = 생일 축하 / present = 선물

korean: 승진 축하해 진짜 대단하다!
english: Congratulations on the promotion! That's amazing!
alt: Way to go on the promotion! You totally deserve it.
note: promotion = 승진 / deserve = 자격이 있다

korean: 결혼 축하해 행복하게 살아!
english: Congratulations on your wedding! Wishing you a happy life!
alt: Happy wedding day! Cheers to a lifetime of happiness!
note: wedding = 결혼 / cheers to = ~를 축하하며

korean: 합격했다고? 대박! 축하해!
english: You passed? That's awesome! Congrats!
alt: You got in? No way! That's incredible! Congrats!
note: passed = 합격하다 / congrats = 축하해

korean: 졸업 축하해 이제 사회인이네!
english: Congratulations on graduating! You're a real adult now!
alt: Happy graduation! Welcome to the real world!
note: graduating = 졸업 / the real world = 사회

korean: 출산 축하해! 엄마 아기 모두 건강하지?
english: Congrats on the baby! Are mom and baby both healthy?
alt: Congrats on the new addition! Everyone doing well?
note: baby = 아기 / new addition = 새 식구

korean: 진짜 미안해 내가 잘못했어
english: I'm really sorry. It was my fault.
alt: I sincerely apologize. I was in the wrong.
note: my fault = 내 잘못 / in the wrong = 잘못한

korean: 오해가 있었어 그런 뜻이 아니었어
english: There was a misunderstanding. That's not what I meant.
alt: I think there's been a mix-up. I didn't mean it that way.
note: misunderstanding = 오해 / mix-up = 혼동

korean: 화내서 미안 내가 너무 예민했어
english: Sorry for getting angry. I was being too sensitive.
alt: I overreacted. My bad.
note: sensitive = 예민한 / overreacted = 과잉 반응했다

korean: 약속 깜빡해서 정말 미안해
english: I'm so sorry I forgot about our plans.
alt: I totally blanked on our plans. I feel terrible.
note: forgot = 깜빡하다 / blanked = 잊어버리다

korean: 다시는 안 그럴게 진심이야
english: I won't do it again. I promise.
alt: It won't happen again. You have my word.
note: I promise = 약속해 / you have my word = 내 말 믿어

korean: 어젯밤에 잠을 못 잤어 뒤척이기만 했어
english: I couldn't sleep last night. I just tossed and turned.
alt: I was up all night. Couldn't fall asleep no matter what.
note: tossed and turned = 뒤척이다

korean: 알람 듣고도 이불 밖으로 못 나왔어
english: I heard the alarm but couldn't get out from under the covers.
alt: The alarm went off but I was glued to the bed.
note: alarm = 알람 / glued to the bed = 일어나지 못한

korean: 낮잠 한 시간 잤더니 개운해
english: I took a one-hour nap and I feel so refreshed.
alt: A quick power nap did wonders for me.
note: nap = 낮잠 / refreshed = 개운한

korean: 자기 전에 핸드폰 보면 잠이 안 와
english: If I look at my phone before bed I can't fall asleep.
alt: Screen time before bed totally ruins my sleep.
note: screen time = 스크린 타임 / ruins = 망치다

korean: 코골이가 심해서 같이 자기 힘들어
english: The snoring is so bad it's hard to sleep together.
alt: Sleeping next to a snorer is basically impossible.
note: snoring = 코골이

korean: 어젯밤 꿈이 너무 생생했어
english: I had the most vivid dream last night.
alt: My dream last night felt so real it was crazy.
note: vivid = 생생한 / felt real = 현실 같았다

korean: 잠이 부족하면 집중이 안 돼
english: When I'm sleep-deprived I can't focus.
alt: Lack of sleep kills my concentration.
note: sleep-deprived = 수면 부족인 / concentration = 집중력

korean: 주말에 몰아 자도 피곤함이 안 풀려
english: Even sleeping in on weekends doesn't fix my fatigue.
alt: Catching up on sleep over the weekend doesn't actually work.
note: sleeping in = 늦잠 자다 / fatigue = 피곤함

korean: 멜라토닌 먹어볼까 잠이 안 와서
english: Should I try melatonin? I've been having trouble sleeping.
alt: Maybe melatonin would help with my insomnia.
note: melatonin = 멜라토닌 / insomnia = 불면증

korean: 이불 속이 최고야 나가기 싫어
english: Under the covers is the best. I don't want to get out.
alt: My bed is my happy place. Leaving is not an option.
note: under the covers = 이불 속 / happy place = 최고의 장소

korean: 요즘 스트레스 받아서 머리가 아파
english: I've been so stressed lately I'm getting headaches.
alt: Stress is literally giving me headaches these days.
note: stressed = 스트레스 받는 / headaches = 두통

korean: 일이 너무 많아서 번아웃 올 것 같아
english: There's so much work I think I'm heading for burnout.
alt: I'm on the verge of burnout from the workload.
note: burnout = 번아웃 / workload = 업무량

korean: 스트레스 풀려면 뭐 해야 할까?
english: What should I do to relieve stress?
alt: How do you de-stress? I need ideas.
note: relieve stress = 스트레스 풀다 / de-stress = 스트레스 해소

korean: 상사가 또 잔소리해서 진짜 짜증나
english: My boss nagged me again and I'm so annoyed.
alt: The boss is on my case again. So frustrating.
note: nagged = 잔소리하다 / on my case = 잔소리하는

korean: 명상 시작했는데 확실히 마음이 편해져
english: I started meditating and it definitely calms me down.
alt: Meditation has been a game changer for my mental health.
note: meditating = 명상 / calms me down = 마음이 편해지다

korean: 요즘 잠도 못 자고 밥도 못 먹어
english: I can't sleep or eat these days.
alt: I've lost my appetite and my sleep is all messed up.
note: lost appetite = 식욕 없는 / messed up = 엉망인

korean: 가끔은 혼자만의 시간이 필요해
english: Sometimes I just need time to myself.
alt: I need some me-time every now and then.
note: time to myself = 혼자만의 시간 / me-time = 개인 시간

korean: 운동하면 스트레스 확실히 풀려
english: Working out definitely helps me deal with stress.
alt: Exercise is my go-to stress reliever.
note: working out = 운동 / stress reliever = 스트레스 해소제

korean: 취미 생활이 없으니까 스트레스가 쌓여
english: Without hobbies my stress just keeps building up.
alt: Not having a hobby makes the stress pile up.
note: hobbies = 취미 / building up = 쌓이다

korean: 다이어트 시작했는데 3일 만에 포기했어
english: I started a diet but gave up after 3 days.
alt: My diet lasted all of three days before I caved.
note: gave up = 포기하다 / caved = 무너지다

korean: 탄수화물 줄이려고 하는데 너무 힘들어
english: I'm trying to cut carbs but it's so hard.
alt: Cutting carbs is the hardest thing I've ever done.
note: cut carbs = 탄수화물 줄이다

korean: 야식 먹으면 안 되는데 자꾸 먹게 돼
english: I know I shouldn't eat late at night but I keep doing it.
alt: Late-night snacking is my biggest weakness.
note: late-night snacking = 야식 / weakness = 약점

korean: 살 빼려면 먹는 양을 줄여야 해
english: To lose weight you need to eat less.
alt: Portion control is key to weight loss.
note: eat less = 적게 먹다 / portion control = 식단 조절

korean: 간헐적 단식 해보고 있어 16대8으로
english: I'm trying intermittent fasting on a 16:8 schedule.
alt: I've been doing 16:8 intermittent fasting.
note: intermittent fasting = 간헐적 단식

korean: 체중계 올라가기 무서워
english: I'm scared to step on the scale.
alt: The scale is my worst enemy right now.
note: step on the scale = 체중계에 올라가다

korean: 살찐 게 아니라 부은 거야
english: I'm not fat I'm just bloated.
alt: It's not weight gain. I'm just retaining water.
note: bloated = 부은 / retaining water = 부종

korean: 치팅데이라서 마음껏 먹을 거야
english: It's my cheat day so I'm eating whatever I want.
alt: Cheat day! No guilt today.
note: cheat day = 치팅데이 / no guilt = 죄책감 없이

korean: 헬스장 다니면서 식단 관리하니까 효과 있어
english: Going to the gym and managing my diet together, I can see results.
alt: Combining workouts with meal prep is really paying off.
note: managing diet = 식단 관리 / paying off = 효과가 있다

korean: 요즘 샐러드만 먹으니까 입이 심심해
english: I've been eating only salads and my mouth is bored.
alt: Living on salads is so bland. I miss real food.
note: bland = 심심한 / miss = 그립다

korean: 이 영화 진짜 재밌다 강추!
english: This movie is amazing. Highly recommend!
alt: You HAVE to see this movie. It's a must-watch.
note: highly recommend = 강추 / must-watch = 꼭 봐야 할

korean: 스포일러 하지 마 아직 안 봤어!
english: No spoilers! I haven't watched it yet!
alt: Don't spoil it for me! I'm still catching up!
note: spoilers = 스포일러 / catching up = 따라잡는 중

korean: 넷플릭스에서 뭐 볼 만한 거 있어?
english: Is there anything good to watch on Netflix?
alt: Any Netflix recommendations?
note: recommendations = 추천

korean: 이 드라마 몰아보느라 밤새웠어
english: I stayed up all night binge-watching this drama.
alt: I pulled an all-nighter binging this K-drama.
note: binge-watching = 몰아보기 / all-nighter = 밤샘

korean: 결말이 허무해서 좀 실망했어
english: The ending was anticlimactic and I was kind of disappointed.
alt: What a letdown of an ending. I expected more.
note: anticlimactic = 허무한 / letdown = 실망

korean: OST가 너무 좋아 계속 듣고 있어
english: The OST is so good I keep listening to it on repeat.
alt: I can't stop replaying the soundtrack from that show.
note: OST = 오리지널 사운드트랙 / on repeat = 반복해서

korean: 주인공 연기 진짜 잘한다
english: The lead actor's performance is incredible.
alt: The main character absolutely nailed the role.
note: performance = 연기 / nailed = 완벽하게 해내다

korean: 이 영화 극장에서 봐야 해 스크린이 다르잖아
english: You should see this in theaters. The big screen hits different.
alt: This one deserves the cinema experience. Trust me.
note: theaters = 극장 / big screen = 큰 화면

korean: 시즌 2 나온대 기대된다
english: They're making a season 2. I'm so excited.
alt: Season 2 just got confirmed. Can't wait.
note: season 2 = 시즌 2 / can't wait = 기대된다

korean: 팝콘 사야지 영화 볼 때 필수지
english: Gotta get popcorn. It's essential for watching movies.
alt: Movie without popcorn? Not happening.
note: essential = 필수인 / popcorn = 팝콘

korean: 이 노래 제목이 뭐야? 너무 좋다
english: What's the name of this song? It's so good.
alt: What track is this? I love it.
note: track = 곡 / song = 노래

korean: 요즘 이 노래 무한 반복 중이야
english: I've had this song on repeat nonstop lately.
alt: This track has been stuck in my playlist for weeks.
note: on repeat = 반복 / nonstop = 쉬지 않고

korean: 콘서트 티켓 구했어! 진짜 운 좋다
english: I got concert tickets! I'm so lucky!
alt: Scored concert tickets! I can't believe my luck!

english: It's a bit bland. I should add more salt.

alt: Using a meal kit makes cooking a breeze.

alt: This is way too salty. I need water.

alt: I hate bathroom duty more than anything.

alt: Left the wash in the machine too long. Gotta run it again.

korean: 창문 닦으니까 확실히 밝아졌어

alt: The room looks so much brighter after washing the windows.

alt: My robot vacuum does all the work. Love it.

alt: We're moving at the start of next month. Date is set.

alt: Sorting through everything for the move is exhausting.
note: packing = 짐 싸기 / sorting through = 정리하다

korean: 이사업체 비교해봤어 견적 차이 많이 나

alt: Got estimates from a few movers. Prices vary a lot.
note: moving companies = 이사업체 / quotes = 견적

korean: 짐 버릴 거 많아 중고로 팔까?

alt: There's so much to toss. Maybe I should sell some of it online.
note: get rid of = 버리다 / secondhand = 중고

alt: Still deciding on the interior design for the new apartment.
note: decorate = 인테리어하다 / interior design = 인테리어

alt: Gotta update my residence registration at the district office.
note: register address = 전입신고 / community center = 주민센터

alt: We brought dduk to the neighbors as a moving-in greeting.

alt: Utility costs went up. Must be the heating eating it up.
note: maintenance fee = 관리비 / heating bill = 난방비

korean: 짐 풀려면 일주일은 걸릴 것 같아
english: I think it'll take at least a week to unpack.
alt: Unpacking is gonna take a solid week at least.
note: unpack = 짐 풀다 / at least = 최소한

korean: 원룸에서 투룸으로 옮기니까 넓어서 좋다
english: Moving from a studio to a two-room is so nice because of the space.
alt: Upgrading from a studio to a 1BR feels like a palace.
note: studio = 원룸 / two-room = 투룸 / 1BR = 방 하나짜리

korean: 우리 강아지 사료 바꿨더니 잘 먹어
english: I switched my dog's food and he's eating so well.
alt: Changed the pup's kibble and he loves it.
note: switched food = 사료 바꾸다 / kibble = 사료

korean: 고양이가 창밖 새 보면서 꼬리 흔들어
english: My cat watches birds through the window and wags its tail.
alt: The cat goes nuts watching birds from the windowsill.

korean: 반려동물 동반 카페 찾았어 같이 갈래?
english: I found a pet-friendly cafe. Want to go together?
alt: There's a pet cafe nearby. Wanna check it out?
note: pet-friendly = 반려동물 동반 / pet cafe = 펫카페

korean: 오늘 유산소 30분 했어 뿌듯해
english: I did 30 minutes of cardio today. Feeling proud.
alt: Got in a solid 30-minute cardio session. Feels great.
note: cardio = 유산소 / feeling proud = 뿌듯한

korean: 운동 파트너 구하면 동기부여가 돼
english: Having a workout partner keeps me motivated.
alt: A gym buddy makes all the difference for motivation.
note: workout partner = 운동 파트너 / motivated = 동기부여 된

korean: 필라테스 시작했는데 코어가 단련되는 느낌이야
english: I started Pilates and I can feel my core getting stronger.
alt: Pilates is amazing for core strength. I feel the difference already.
note: Pilates = 필라테스 / core = 코어

english: I'm growing my hair out but my bangs keep getting in the way.
alt: Trying to grow out my hair but the awkward phase is real.
note: growing out = 기르는 중 / awkward phase = 어중간한 시기

korean: 두피 관리 받으러 가야겠다 비듬이 심해
english: I need to go get scalp treatment. My dandruff is bad.
alt: Dandruff is out of control. Time for a scalp care session.
note: scalp treatment = 두피 관리 / dandruff = 비듬

korean: 버스카드 잔액 부족이래 충전해야 해
english: My bus card says insufficient balance. I need to recharge it.
alt: My transit card is out. Gotta top it up.
note: bus card = 교통카드 / recharge = 충전하다 / top up = 충전

korean: 전화번호 바꿨어 새 번호로 저장해줘
english: I changed my phone number. Save my new one.
alt: Got a new number. Update your contacts.
note: phone number = 전화번호 / save = 저장하다

korean: 국제전화 요금이 비싸서 카톡으로 통화해
english: International calls are expensive so I use KakaoTalk.
alt: Overseas calling costs a fortune. Let's just KakaoTalk.
note: international calls = 국제전화 / KakaoTalk = 카카오톡

korean: 반품하려는데 택배비 누가 부담해요?
english: Who pays for the return shipping?
alt: Is the return postage on me or the seller?
note: return shipping = 반품 택배비 / postage = 우편요금

korean: 새벽배송으로 시키면 아침에 도착해
english: If you order dawn delivery it arrives by morning.
alt: Early morning delivery gets it there before breakfast.
note: dawn delivery = 새벽배송

korean: 통장 잔고가 바닥이야 월급 빨리 들어와라
english: My bank balance is rock bottom. I need payday to come faster.
alt: My account is bone dry. Can't wait for payday.
note: bank balance = 통장 잔고 / payday = 월급날

korean: 가계부 쓰기 시작했어 지출 관리 해야 해
english: I started keeping a household ledger. I need to manage my spending.
alt: I'm tracking expenses now. Gotta get my finances in order.
note: household ledger = 가계부 / expenses = 지출

english: I hate going to the dentist. Even the sound of the drill scares me.
alt: The dentist's drill gives me nightmares.
note: dentist = 치과 / drill = 드릴

korean: 처방전 들고 약국 가서 약 타야 해
english: I need to take my prescription to the pharmacy and pick up my medication.
alt: Gotta swing by the pharmacy with my prescription.
note: prescription = 처방전 / pharmacy = 약국

korean: 카페라떼 따뜻한 걸로 주세요 시럽 빼고요
english: A hot cafe latte please without syrup.
alt: Can I have a warm latte with no syrup?
note: latte = 라떼 / without syrup = 시럽 없이

korean: 여기 콘센트 있는 자리 있나요?
english: Do you have any seats with outlets?
alt: Are there any spots with power sockets?
note: outlets = 콘센트 / power sockets = 전기 콘센트

korean: 술 안 마셔도 분위기는 즐길 수 있어
english: You can enjoy the atmosphere even without drinking.
alt: You don't have to drink to have a good time.
note: atmosphere = 분위기 / have a good time = 즐기다

korean: 안주 없이 술만 마시면 빨리 취해

korean: 윗집에서 물이 새 관리실에 연락해야겠다
english: Water is leaking from upstairs. I need to contact the management office.
alt: There's a leak from upstairs. Calling maintenance right away.
note: leaking = 새다 / management office = 관리실

korean: 아파트 반상회 참석해야 하나 고민이야
english: I'm debating whether to attend the apartment residents' meeting.
alt: Should I go to the building meeting? Not sure if it's worth it.
note: residents' meeting = 반상회 / attend = 참석하다

korean: 날씨 풀렸다 드디어 봄이 왔나 봐
english: The weather warmed up. I think spring is finally here.
alt: It's warming up. Looks like spring has arrived at last.
note: warmed up = 풀리다 / spring = 봄

korean: 한여름 폭염에는 밖에 나가기 싫어
english: I don't want to go outside during the midsummer heat wave.
alt: The summer heatwave makes me want to stay indoors forever.
note: heat wave = 폭염 / midsummer = 한여름
