english: The Lunar New Year holiday is short this year. Too bad.
alt: Wish the Seollal break were longer this year.
note: Lunar New Year = 설날 / break = 연휴

korean: 명절 때 전 부치는 냄새가 온 집에 퍼져
english: The smell of pan-frying jeon fills the whole house during holidays.
alt: The whole apartment smells like sizzling jeon on holiday mornings.
note: jeon = 전 / pan-frying = 부치기 / sizzling = 지글지글

korean: 1주년 축하해 앞으로도 잘하자!
english: Happy 1st anniversary! Let's keep it going!
alt: Cheers to one year! Here's to many more!
note: anniversary = 기념일 / here's to = ~를 위하여

korean: 당선 축하드립니다 고생 많으셨습니다
english: Congratulations on your election. You've worked so hard.
alt: Well-deserved win. Congratulations on the election.
note: election = 당선 / well-deserved = 당연한

korean: 늦게 답장해서 미안 바빴어
english: Sorry for the late reply. I was busy.
alt: Apologies for the slow response. Things got hectic.
note: late reply = 늦은 답장 / hectic = 정신없는

korean: 실수로 보낸 거야 무시해줘
english: I sent that by accident. Please ignore it.
alt: That was a mistake. Just disregard it.
note: by accident = 실수로 / disregard = 무시하다

korean: 잠이 보약이야 푹 자야 해
english: Sleep is the best medicine. You need to rest well.
alt: Nothing beats a good night's sleep for healing.
note: sleep is the best medicine = 잠이 보약 / healing = 치유

korean: 새벽에 눈이 떠져서 다시 잠들기 힘들어
english: I wake up in the middle of the night and can't fall back asleep.
alt: I keep waking up at dawn and lying there wide awake.
note: wake up = 눈이 떠지다 / wide awake = 완전히 깬

korean: 직장 스트레스 때문에 이직 고민 중이야
english: Work stress is making me consider changing jobs.
alt: The stress at work has me thinking about a career move.
note: changing jobs = 이직 / career move = 커리어 전환

korean: 심호흡 하면 좀 진정돼 해봐
english: Taking deep breaths helps you calm down. Try it.
alt: Try some deep breathing. It really settles the nerves.
note: deep breaths = 심호흡 / calm down = 진정하다

korean: 요요 왔어 다시 원래 체중으로 돌아갔어
english: I yo-yo'd. I'm back to my original weight.
alt: The weight bounced right back. Classic yo-yo.
note: yo-yo = 요요 현상 / bounced back = 되돌아오다

korean: 단백질 위주로 먹으려고 해 닭가슴살 질렸어
english: I'm trying to eat more protein but I'm sick of chicken breast.
alt: Protein-heavy diet means chicken breast again. So over it.
note: protein = 단백질 / chicken breast = 닭가슴살 / sick of = 질린

korean: 이 영화 반전 있어 끝까지 봐야 해
english: This movie has a twist. You have to watch till the end.
alt: Stick with it. The plot twist at the end is worth it.
note: twist = 반전 / stick with it = 끝까지 보다

korean: 감독판은 극장판이랑 다르대 더 길어
english: The director's cut is different from the theatrical version. It's longer.
alt: Apparently the director's cut has extra scenes not in theaters.
note: director's cut = 감독판 / theatrical version = 극장판

korean: 이 노래 가사가 와닿아 요즘 내 상황 같아
english: The lyrics of this song really hit home. It feels like my situation.
alt: These lyrics speak to me. It's like they wrote it about my life.
note: lyrics = 가사 / hit home = 와닿다

korean: 악기 하나는 배워두면 좋아 취미로
english: It's good to learn at least one instrument as a hobby.
alt: Pick up an instrument. It's a hobby that never gets old.
note: instrument = 악기 / never gets old = 질리지 않는

korean: 이 게임 중독성 있어 못 끊겠어
english: This game is so addictive I can't stop playing.
alt: I'm hooked on this game. It's dangerously addictive.
note: addictive = 중독성 있는 / hooked = 빠진

korean: 같이 협동 모드 하자 혼자 하면 재미없어
english: Let's play co-op mode. It's no fun playing solo.
alt: Join me for co-op. Solo is boring.
note: co-op mode = 협동 모드 / solo = 혼자

korean: 밑줄 치면서 읽으면 기억에 남아
english: Underlining while you read helps you remember better.
alt: Highlighting key passages makes them stick in your mind.
note: underlining = 밑줄 치기 / stick = 기억에 남다

korean: 자기계발서 추천해줘 동기부여 되는 거
english: Recommend me a self-help book. Something motivational.
alt: Know any good personal development books? I need some inspiration.
note: self-help book = 자기계발서 / motivational = 동기부여 되는

korean: 찌개 끓일 때 된장 얼마나 넣어?
english: How much doenjang do you put in when making stew?
alt: What's the right amount of soybean paste for the jjigae?
note: doenjang = 된장 / soybean paste = 된장

korean: 볶음밥은 찬밥으로 해야 맛있어
english: Fried rice tastes better with leftover rice.
alt: Use cold rice for fried rice. That's the secret.
note: fried rice = 볶음밥 / leftover rice = 찬밥

korean: 배수구 막혀서 물이 안 빠져
english: The drain is clogged and the water won't go down.
alt: Drain's blocked. Water is just sitting there.
note: drain = 배수구 / clogged = 막힌

korean: 빨래 개는 거 세상에서 제일 귀찮아
english: Folding laundry is the most annoying thing in the world.
alt: I'd rather do anything than fold laundry.
note: folding laundry = 빨래 개기 / annoying = 귀찮은

korean: 보증금 돌려받으려면 깨끗하게 써야 해
english: You need to keep the place clean to get your deposit back.
alt: Keep the apartment in good condition if you want your deposit returned.
note: deposit = 보증금 / in good condition = 깨끗하게

korean: 이삿짐 센터 불러야 하나 셀프로 할까
english: Should I hire movers or do it myself?
alt: Debating between professional movers and a DIY move.
note: hire movers = 이삿짐 센터 부르다 / DIY = 셀프

korean: 냥이가 스크래처 안 쓰고 소파만 긁어
english: My cat ignores the scratcher and only scratches the sofa.
alt: The cat's scratching post is untouched. The couch isn't so lucky.
note: scratcher = 스크래처 / scratches = 긁다

korean: 산책 나갔더니 비가 와서 강아지가 젖었어
english: It started raining on our walk and the dog got soaked.
alt: Got caught in the rain during our walk. Puppy is drenched.
note: soaked = 젖은 / drenched = 흠뻑 젖은

korean: 런닝머신보다 밖에서 뛰는 게 좋아
english: I prefer running outside to using a treadmill.
alt: Outdoor running beats the treadmill any day.
note: treadmill = 런닝머신 / outdoor running = 야외 달리기

korean: 운동 전에 워밍업 꼭 해야 부상 안 당해
english: You must warm up before exercising to avoid injuries.
alt: Always warm up first. Skipping it is how you get hurt.
note: warm up = 워밍업 / injuries = 부상

korean: 속눈썹 연장했는데 자연스러워서 좋아
english: I got eyelash extensions and they look so natural.
alt: My lash extensions turned out super natural-looking.
note: eyelash extensions = 속눈썹 연장 / natural = 자연스러운

korean: 피부 톤 밝아지는 세럼 추천해줘
english: Recommend a serum that brightens skin tone.
alt: Know any good brightening serums?
note: serum = 세럼 / brightens = 밝게 하다

korean: KTX 예매했어 자유석밖에 없대
english: I booked the KTX but only unreserved seats were available.
alt: Got KTX tickets but had to settle for standing room.
note: KTX = 고속열차 / unreserved seats = 자유석

korean: 전동킥보드 헬멧 써야 돼 안 쓰면 벌금이야
english: You have to wear a helmet on electric scooters or you'll get fined.
alt: Helmet is mandatory for e-scooters. Fines are no joke.
note: helmet = 헬멧 / mandatory = 의무적인

korean: ARS 연결 대기 시간 너무 길어
english: The ARS hold time is way too long.
alt: I've been on hold forever. This automated system is the worst.
note: hold time = 대기 시간 / automated system = 자동 시스템

korean: 음성 메시지 남길게요 확인 후 연락 주세요
english: I'll leave a voicemail. Please call me back when you check it.
alt: I'll leave a message. Get back to me when you can.
note: voicemail = 음성 메시지 / call back = 다시 전화하다

korean: 묶음 배송해주세요 따로 오면 불편해요
english: Please bundle the shipment. It's inconvenient if they come separately.
alt: Can you combine the orders into one delivery?
note: bundle = 묶음 / combine = 합치다

korean: 냉동 식품은 아이스박스 포장해주나요?
english: Do frozen items come in an insulated box?
alt: Is there cold packaging for the frozen stuff?
note: insulated box = 아이스박스 / cold packaging = 냉동 포장

korean: 환율 좋을 때 환전해놓으려고
english: I want to exchange currency while the rate is good.
alt: Planning to buy some foreign currency while the rate is favorable.
note: exchange currency = 환전 / rate = 환율

korean: 신용등급 관리해야 대출 받기 쉬워
english: You need to maintain a good credit score to get loans easily.
alt: Keep your credit score up. It makes borrowing so much easier.
note: credit score = 신용등급 / borrowing = 대출

korean: 접수하셨으면 대기실에서 기다려주세요
english: If you've checked in please wait in the waiting room.
alt: Once you're registered please have a seat in the waiting area.
note: checked in = 접수하다 / waiting room = 대기실

korean: 수술 후 회복 기간이 얼마나 걸려요?
english: How long is the recovery period after surgery?
alt: What's the expected recovery time post-surgery?
note: recovery period = 회복 기간 / surgery = 수술

korean: 카페 단골이라 바리스타가 내 취향 알아
english: I'm a regular so the barista knows my preferences.
alt: I go there so often the barista starts making my drink before I order.
note: regular = 단골 / barista = 바리스타

korean: 드립 커피 한 잔 주세요 원두 뭐 있어요?
english: One drip coffee please. What beans do you have?
alt: Can I get a pour-over? What origins are available?
note: drip coffee = 드립 커피 / beans = 원두

korean: 막걸리 한 잔 하면서 파전 먹자
english: Let's have makgeolli with pajeon.
alt: How about some rice wine and Korean pancake?
note: makgeolli = 막걸리 / pajeon = 파전

korean: 술 마시고 필름 끊겼어 뭐 했는지 모르겠어
english: I blacked out from drinking. I have no idea what I did.
alt: Drank so much I blacked out. Complete memory loss.
note: blacked out = 필름 끊기다 / memory loss = 기억 상실

korean: 이웃 강아지가 자꾸 우리 집 앞에서 짖어
english: The neighbor's dog keeps barking in front of our house.
alt: The dog next door won't stop barking outside our door.
note: neighbor's dog = 이웃 강아지 / barking = 짖다

korean: 첫눈 왔다! 나가서 보자
english: It's the first snow! Let's go outside and see it.
alt: First snow of the year! Come look!
note: first snow = 첫눈

korean: 추석에 한복 입을 거야 오랜만이야
english: I'm wearing hanbok for Chuseok. It's been a while.
alt: Going traditional this Chuseok. Bringing out the hanbok.
note: hanbok = 한복

korean: 새해 복 많이 받으세요!
english: Happy New Year! Wishing you all the best.
alt: May the new year bring you happiness and prosperity.
note: Happy New Year = 새해 복 많이 받으세요

korean: 일부러 그런 거 아니야 이해해줘
english: I didn't do it on purpose. Please understand.
alt: It wasn't intentional. Bear with me.
note: on purpose = 일부러 / intentional = 의도적인

korean: 수면 패턴이 완전 망가졌어 밤낮이 바뀌었어
english: My sleep schedule is completely messed up. I've got my days and nights mixed up.
alt: My circadian rhythm is destroyed. I'm nocturnal now.
note: sleep schedule = 수면 패턴 / circadian rhythm = 생체 리듬

korean: 주말에 아무것도 안 하고 쉬어야 충전돼
english: I need to do absolutely nothing on weekends to recharge.
alt: Weekends are for doing zilch. That's how I reset.
note: recharge = 충전하다 / zilch = 아무것도

korean: 공복 운동이 다이어트에 좋대 진짜야?
english: They say working out on an empty stomach is good for dieting. Is that true?
alt: Is fasted cardio really better for weight loss?
note: empty stomach = 공복 / fasted cardio = 공복 유산소

korean: 자막 없이 영화 보는 게 목표야
english: My goal is to watch movies without subtitles.
alt: I want to get to the point where I don't need subs.
note: subtitles = 자막 / subs = 자막 (줄임말)

korean: 버스킹 보러 홍대 갈래?
english: Want to go to Hongdae to watch busking?
alt: Let's catch some street performances in Hongdae.
note: busking = 버스킹 / street performances = 거리 공연

korean: 세이브 안 하고 껐다가 다 날아갔어
english: I turned it off without saving and lost everything.
alt: Forgot to save before quitting. All my progress is gone.
note: saving = 저장 / progress = 진행 상황

korean: 추리 소설 좋아해? 요즘 빠져 있어
english: Do you like mystery novels? I'm really into them lately.
alt: I've been hooked on mystery fiction these days. Any recs?
note: mystery novels = 추리 소설 / hooked on = 빠진

korean: 파스타 삶을 때 소금 넣어야 간이 배
english: You need to add salt when boiling pasta so it absorbs flavor.
alt: Salt the pasta water generously. It makes all the difference.
note: boiling = 삶다 / absorbs flavor = 간이 배다

korean: 환기 시키자 공기가 탁해
english: Let's air out the room. The air is stuffy.
alt: Open a window. It's stuffy in here.
note: air out = 환기하다 / stuffy = 탁한

korean: 전세 만기라서 이사 가야 할 것 같아
english: My lease is expiring so I might have to move.
alt: The jeonse contract is up. Looks like I'm moving.
note: lease = 전세 / expiring = 만기 되는

korean: 강아지가 배 보여주면서 누우면 쓰다듬어달라는 거야
english: When your dog rolls over and shows its belly it wants belly rubs.
alt: If the pup flops over belly-up that's a clear request for scratches.
note: rolls over = 뒤집어 눕다 / belly rubs = 배 쓰다듬기

korean: 동물 등록 했어? 마이크로칩 넣어야 해
english: Did you register your pet? You need to get a microchip.
alt: Have you microchipped your pet? It's required for registration.
note: register = 등록하다 / microchip = 마이크로칩

korean: 고양이 중성화 수술 시켜야 건강에 좋대
english: They say neutering your cat is better for its health.
alt: Spaying or neutering is recommended for cats. Healthier long-term.
note: neutering = 중성화 / spaying = 중성화 (암컷)

korean: 벤치프레스 자세 교정 받았더니 확실히 다르다
english: After getting my bench press form corrected it feels totally different.
alt: A form check on bench made a huge difference in my lifts.
note: bench press = 벤치프레스 / form = 자세

korean: 오늘 만보 걸었어 목표 달성!
english: I walked 10000 steps today. Goal achieved!
alt: Hit my 10K step goal today. Nailed it!
note: steps = 걸음 / goal achieved = 목표 달성

korean: 크로스핏 해봤는데 죽을 뻔했어
english: I tried CrossFit and nearly died.
alt: CrossFit almost killed me. Never again. Or maybe again.
note: CrossFit = 크로스핏 / nearly died = 죽을 뻔했다

korean: 배드민턴 치러 갈래? 코트 예약했어
english: Want to play badminton? I booked a court.
alt: I reserved a badminton court. You down to play?
note: badminton = 배드민턴 / booked a court = 코트 예약

korean: 눈썹 정리하니까 인상이 달라졌어
english: Grooming my eyebrows totally changed my look.
alt: Getting my brows shaped made a huge difference.
note: eyebrows = 눈썹 / grooming = 정리하기

korean: 페이셜 에센스 뭐 써? 추천 좀
english: What facial essence do you use? Any recommendations?
alt: What's your go-to essence? I need a new one.
note: facial essence = 에센스 / go-to = 애용하는

korean: 탈모 걱정돼서 탈모 샴푸 쓰고 있어
english: I'm worried about hair loss so I'm using an anti-hair-loss shampoo.
alt: Started using hair growth shampoo. Hoping it actually works.
note: hair loss = 탈모 / anti-hair-loss = 탈모 방지

korean: 교통체증에 꼼짝 못 하고 있어
english: I'm stuck in traffic and can't move at all.
alt: Completely gridlocked. Not going anywhere anytime soon.
note: stuck in traffic = 교통체증에 갇힌 / gridlocked = 정체된

korean: 우회전할 때 보행자 먼저야 조심해
english: Pedestrians have the right of way when turning right. Be careful.
alt: Watch for pedestrians on your right turn. They have priority.
note: pedestrians = 보행자 / right of way = 우선권

korean: 전화 스피커폰으로 할게 양해해줘
english: I'm putting you on speakerphone if that's okay.
alt: Mind if I put you on speaker?
note: speakerphone = 스피커폰 / mind if = 괜찮겠어?

korean: 통화 녹음해도 될까? 중요한 내용이라서
english: Can I record this call? It's important information.
alt: Do you mind if I record? I don't want to miss anything.
note: record = 녹음하다 / important = 중요한

korean: 택배 경비실에서 안 찾아가면 반송된대
english: If you don't pick up the package from the security desk they'll send it back.
alt: Unclaimed packages at the front desk get returned after a while.
note: pick up = 찾아가다 / returned = 반송된

korean: 로켓 프레시로 시키면 신선도가 좋아
english: Rocket Fresh delivery keeps things really fresh.
alt: Fresh produce from Rocket delivery is surprisingly good quality.
note: Rocket Fresh = 로켓프레시 / fresh = 신선한

korean: 적금 금리 비교해봤는데 인터넷뱅크가 높아
english: I compared savings interest rates and online banks are higher.
alt: Online banks offer way better rates on fixed deposits.
note: interest rates = 금리 / online banks = 인터넷뱅크

korean: 체크카드 해외 결제 수수료 얼마야?
english: What's the international transaction fee on my debit card?
alt: How much is the foreign currency fee on this card?
note: international transaction fee = 해외 결제 수수료

korean: 감기약 먹으면 졸리니까 운전 조심해
english: Cold medicine makes you drowsy so be careful driving.
alt: OTC cold meds cause drowsiness. Don't drive after taking them.
note: cold medicine = 감기약 / drowsy = 졸린

korean: 물리치료 받으러 다니고 있어 허리 때문에
english: I've been going to physical therapy for my back.
alt: I'm in PT for my lower back issues.
note: physical therapy = 물리치료 / lower back = 허리

korean: 디저트 세트 있어요? 음료랑 같이 할인되는 거
english: Do you have a dessert set? Like a combo with a drink?
alt: Is there a drink-and-dessert combo deal?
note: dessert set = 디저트 세트 / combo = 세트 메뉴

korean: 카페에서 공부하면 집중이 잘 돼
english: I focus better when I study at a cafe.
alt: Studying at a cafe helps me concentrate.
note: focus = 집중하다 / concentrate = 집중

korean: 맥주 한 잔으로 시작하자 가볍게
english: Let's start light with a beer.
alt: How about we kick things off with just a beer?
note: start light = 가볍게 시작하다 / kick off = 시작하다

korean: 어제 술 마시고 폰 잃어버렸어
english: I lost my phone after drinking last night.
alt: Went out drinking and somehow lost my phone.
note: lost phone = 폰 잃어버리다

korean: 층간 소음 줄이려고 매트 깔았어
english: I laid down mats to reduce floor noise.
alt: Put down some soundproof mats to be considerate of neighbors.
note: mats = 매트 / reduce noise = 소음 줄이다

korean: 이웃 아주머니가 반찬 레시피 알려주셨어
english: The neighbor lady shared her side dish recipes with me.
alt: Our neighbor taught me her secret banchan recipe.
note: shared recipes = 레시피 알려주다

korean: 환절기에 감기 조심해 면역력 챙겨
english: Be careful of colds during the season change. Take care of your immune system.
alt: Watch out for seasonal colds. Keep your immunity up.
note: season change = 환절기 / immune system = 면역력

korean: 태풍 온대 내일 외출 자제해야겠다
english: A typhoon is coming. I should avoid going out tomorrow.
alt: Typhoon warning for tomorrow. Better stay indoors.
note: typhoon = 태풍 / avoid going out = 외출 자제

korean: 추석 선물 세트 뭐 보낼까 고민이야
english: I'm trying to decide what Chuseok gift set to send.
alt: What holiday gift set should I send? Can't decide.
note: gift set = 선물 세트 / Chuseok = 추석

korean: 설날에 윷놀이 하면 진짜 재밌어
english: Playing yutnori on New Year's Day is so much fun.
alt: Yutnori is the best part of Seollal celebrations.
note: yutnori = 윷놀이

korean: 개업 축하해! 대박 나길 바라!
english: Congrats on the grand opening! Hope business booms!
alt: Happy opening day! Wishing you massive success!
note: grand opening = 개업 / booms = 대박 나다

korean: 아이 돌잔치 축하해요 건강하게 자라길!
english: Happy first birthday! May your child grow up healthy!
alt: Congrats on the dol! Wishing the little one a bright future!
note: first birthday = 돌잔치 / dol = 돌

korean: 네 마음 상하게 해서 정말 미안해
english: I'm really sorry for hurting your feelings.
alt: I sincerely apologize for upsetting you.
note: hurting feelings = 마음 상하게 하다 / upsetting = 속상하게 하다

korean: 내 잘못 인정할게 변명 안 할게
english: I'll own up to my mistake. No excuses.
alt: I take full responsibility. No excuses from me.
note: own up = 인정하다 / take responsibility = 책임지다

korean: 수면 앱 써봤는데 수면 질 분석해줘서 좋아
english: I tried a sleep app and it's great because it analyzes your sleep quality.
alt: This sleep tracker app is amazing. It breaks down your sleep stages.
note: sleep app = 수면 앱 / sleep quality = 수면 질

korean: 백색소음 틀어놓으면 잠이 잘 와
english: Playing white noise helps me fall asleep.
alt: White noise is my secret to passing out fast.
note: white noise = 백색소음

korean: 마감 스트레스 때문에 손톱 물어뜯어
english: I bite my nails from deadline stress.
alt: The deadline pressure has me chewing my nails again.
note: bite nails = 손톱 물어뜯다 / deadline = 마감

korean: 상담 받아보는 것도 좋을 것 같아
english: I think getting counseling might be a good idea.
alt: Maybe talking to a therapist would help.
note: counseling = 상담 / therapist = 상담사

korean: 체지방률 측정했는데 생각보다 높아
english: I measured my body fat percentage and it's higher than I thought.
alt: Got my body fat tested. The results were a wake-up call.
note: body fat percentage = 체지방률 / wake-up call = 경종

korean: 물만 마셔도 살찌는 체질인 것 같아
english: I feel like I'm the type to gain weight just from drinking water.
alt: I swear I gain weight from breathing. My metabolism is that slow.
note: gain weight = 살찌다 / metabolism = 신진대사

korean: 이 드라마 캐스팅 완벽해
english: The casting for this drama is perfect.
alt: They nailed the casting on this show. Every actor fits.
note: casting = 캐스팅 / nailed = 완벽하게 해내다

korean: 다큐멘터리도 재밌는 거 많아 편견 버려
english: There are a lot of great documentaries. Drop the prejudice.
alt: Documentaries can be just as gripping. Give them a shot.
note: documentary = 다큐멘터리 / gripping = 흥미진진한

korean: 노래 듣다가 갑자기 울컥했어
english: I suddenly got emotional while listening to a song.
alt: A song came on and I teared up out of nowhere.
note: got emotional = 울컥하다 / teared up = 눈물이 나다

korean: 음원 차트가 실시간으로 바뀌니까 재밌어
english: It's fun watching the music charts change in real time.
alt: Real-time chart movement keeps things exciting.
note: music charts = 음원 차트 / real-time = 실시간

korean: DLC 나왔는데 살 만한 가치 있어?
english: The DLC is out. Is it worth buying?
alt: New DLC dropped. Should I cop it or skip it?
note: DLC = 다운로드 콘텐츠 / worth buying = 살 가치 있는

korean: 게임 서버 터져서 접속이 안 돼
english: The game server crashed so I can't log in.
alt: Servers are down. Can't get in at all.
note: server crashed = 서버 터짐 / log in = 접속하다

korean: 완독했어 결말이 여운이 남아
english: I finished the book. The ending left a lasting impression.
alt: Just finished it. The ending really stayed with me.
note: finished = 완독 / lasting impression = 여운

korean: 읽고 싶은 책이 절판이래 중고로 찾아봐야겠다
english: The book I want to read is out of print. I'll have to look for it secondhand.
alt: It's out of print. Guess I'll hunt for a used copy.
note: out of print = 절판 / secondhand = 중고

korean: 칼질이 서툴러서 시간이 오래 걸려
english: I'm slow at chopping so cooking takes forever.
alt: My knife skills are terrible. Everything takes twice as long.
note: chopping = 칼질 / knife skills = 칼질 솜씨

korean: 양념장 만들어놓으면 두고두고 써먹어
english: If you make sauce in advance you can use it for ages.
alt: Batch-prep the seasoning and it lasts you weeks.
note: sauce = 양념장 / batch-prep = 미리 만들다

korean: 욕실 곰팡이 제거제 뿌려야겠다
english: I need to spray mold remover in the bathroom.
alt: Time to hit the bathroom tiles with mold cleaner.
note: mold remover = 곰팡이 제거제

korean: 식기세척기 돌리면 설거지 안 해도 돼
english: If I run the dishwasher I don't have to hand-wash.
alt: The dishwasher saves me from the sink duty.
note: dishwasher = 식기세척기 / hand-wash = 손설거지

korean: 이사 전에 청소 업체 불러야겠다
english: I should call a cleaning service before moving.
alt: Gonna book a move-out cleaning before we leave.
note: cleaning service = 청소 업체 / move-out cleaning = 퇴거 청소

korean: 새 집 벽지 곰팡이 없는지 확인해봐
english: Check the new place's wallpaper for mold.
alt: Make sure there's no mold behind the wallpaper at the new apartment.
note: wallpaper = 벽지 / mold = 곰팡이

korean: 강아지 이빨 닦아줘야 하는데 입을 안 벌려
english: I need to brush my dog's teeth but he won't open his mouth.
alt: The pup clamps his mouth shut whenever I try to brush his teeth.
note: brush teeth = 이빨 닦다 / clamps shut = 꽉 다물다

korean: 고양이가 새벽에 뛰어다녀서 잠을 못 자
english: My cat runs around at dawn so I can't sleep.
alt: The cat does midnight zoomies and it wrecks my sleep.
note: runs around = 뛰어다니다 / zoomies = 갑자기 뛰어다니기

korean: 반려동물 장례 서비스도 있대
english: There are even pet funeral services now.
alt: I heard they have memorial services for pets these days.
note: pet funeral = 반려동물 장례

korean: 데드리프트 폼 잡기 진짜 어렵다
english: Getting the deadlift form right is really hard.
alt: Deadlift technique is so tricky to nail down.
note: deadlift = 데드리프트 / form = 폼 / technique = 기술

korean: 자전거 타다가 체인 빠졌어
english: My bike chain came off while I was riding.
alt: The chain popped off mid-ride. Had to fix it on the spot.
note: chain = 체인 / came off = 빠지다

korean: 줄넘기 다이어트 효과 좋대
english: They say jump rope is great for weight loss.
alt: Jump rope is apparently one of the best fat-burning exercises.
note: jump rope = 줄넘기 / fat-burning = 지방 연소

korean: 헤어 왁스 뭐 써? 추천해줘
english: What hair wax do you use? Recommend one.
alt: Any good hair styling product recs?
note: hair wax = 헤어 왁스 / styling product = 스타일링 제품

korean: 레이저 시술 받을까 고민 중이야
english: I'm thinking about getting laser treatment.
alt: Considering a laser procedure for my skin.
note: laser treatment = 레이저 시술

korean: 손톱 깎아야 하는데 네일 아트 아까워
english: I need to cut my nails but I don't want to ruin the nail art.
alt: My nails need clipping but I'd hate to lose the art.
note: nail art = 네일 아트 / clipping = 깎기

korean: 카풀로 출근하면 기름값 아낄 수 있어
english: Carpooling to work can save on gas money.
alt: Sharing rides to the office cuts fuel costs in half.
note: carpooling = 카풀 / gas money = 기름값

korean: 정기권 끊으면 교통비 절약돼
english: Getting a transit pass saves on transportation costs.
alt: A monthly pass is way cheaper than paying per ride.
note: transit pass = 정기권 / transportation costs = 교통비

korean: 전화 예절 지켜야지 통화 중에 씹지 마
english: Phone etiquette matters. Don't eat while on a call.
alt: It's rude to chew while you're on the phone.
note: phone etiquette = 전화 예절 / chew = 씹다

korean: 자동응답 안내 너무 복잡해 상담원 연결해줘
english: The automated menu is too complicated. Connect me to an agent.
alt: Just get me a real person. This phone tree is endless.
note: automated menu = 자동응답 / agent = 상담원

korean: 선불 택배함에 넣어주세요
english: Please put it in the prepaid locker.
alt: Drop it in the parcel locker if I'm not home.
note: prepaid locker = 선불 택배함 / parcel locker = 택배함

korean: 배달 음식 리뷰 꼭 남겨야 사장님한테 도움 돼
english: Leaving a review really helps the restaurant owner.
alt: Always leave a review for delivery food. It means a lot to the owner.
note: review = 리뷰 / restaurant owner = 사장님

korean: 모바일 뱅킹 비밀번호 바꿔야겠다 보안 때문에
english: I should change my mobile banking password for security.
alt: Time to update my banking app password. Can't be too careful.
note: mobile banking = 모바일 뱅킹 / security = 보안

korean: 연금저축 들어놓으면 세제 혜택 받을 수 있어
english: If you sign up for a pension savings you can get tax benefits.
alt: Pension savings accounts come with nice tax deductions.
note: pension savings = 연금저축 / tax benefits = 세제 혜택

korean: 진단서 떼러 왔어요 보험 청구용이에요
english: I'm here to get a medical certificate for an insurance claim.
alt: I need a doctor's note for my insurance paperwork.
note: medical certificate = 진단서 / insurance claim = 보험 청구

korean: 예방접종 맞으러 왔어요 독감 백신이요
english: I'm here for a vaccination. The flu shot.
alt: Here for my flu vaccine please.
note: vaccination = 예방접종 / flu shot = 독감 백신

korean: 커피 리필 되나요?
english: Can I get a refill?
alt: Are refills available?
note: refill = 리필

korean: 여기 커피가 산미가 강해서 좋아
english: I like the coffee here because it has a strong acidity.
alt: The fruity acidity in their coffee is what keeps me coming back.
note: acidity = 산미 / fruity = 과일 향의

korean: 술 먹으면 얼굴에 다 나타나 숨기기 힘들어
english: When I drink it shows on my face. Hard to hide.
alt: My face is an open book when I drink. Can't hide it at all.
note: shows on my face = 얼굴에 나타나다 / open book = 숨길 수 없는

korean: 안주 추가 주문할게요 여기요!
english: We'd like to order more anju, please!
alt: Can we get another round of snacks over here?
note: order more = 추가 주문 / round of snacks = 안주 한 판

korean: 복도에서 뛰지 말아주세요 아이들한테 말해줘
english: Please tell the kids not to run in the hallway.
alt: Could you ask your children to keep it down in the corridor?
note: hallway = 복도 / keep it down = 조용히 하다

korean: 재활용 분리수거 제대로 안 하면 과태료 나와
english: You'll get fined if you don't sort recycling properly.
alt: Improper recycling can result in penalties. Sort it right.
note: sort recycling = 분리수거 / penalties = 과태료

korean: 겨울에 내복 입으면 난방비 아낄 수 있어
english: Wearing thermal underwear in winter can save on heating costs.
alt: Long johns help you save on the heating bill in winter.
note: thermal underwear = 내복 / heating costs = 난방비

korean: 올해 봄 꽃이 일찍 피었네 지구 온난화 때문인가
english: Spring flowers bloomed early this year. Maybe because of global warming.
alt: The flowers are early this year. Climate change at work.
note: global warming = 지구 온난화 / bloomed = 피었다

korean: 명절 다음 날 피곤해서 아무것도 못 해
english: The day after a holiday I'm so tired I can't do anything.
alt: Post-holiday exhaustion is real. I need a recovery day.
note: exhaustion = 피로 / recovery day = 회복일

korean: 제사 음식 준비하느라 새벽부터 일어났어
english: I woke up at dawn to prepare the ancestral ritual food.
alt: Up before sunrise to prep the jesa food.
note: ancestral ritual = 제사 / dawn = 새벽

korean: 복직 축하해! 다시 돌아와서 좋다
english: Welcome back! Congrats on returning to work!
alt: Glad to have you back. Congrats on the reinstatement!
note: returning to work = 복직 / reinstatement = 복직

korean: 집들이 축하해! 새집 분위기 좋다
english: Happy housewarming! The new place looks great!
alt: Love the new digs! Congrats on the move!
note: housewarming = 집들이 / new digs = 새 집 (속어)

korean: 내가 먼저 사과할게 기분 나빴지?
english: Let me apologize first. I know you were upset.
alt: I'll go first. I'm sorry. I know it bothered you.
note: apologize = 사과하다 / bothered = 기분 나쁜

korean: 그때 그 말은 진심이 아니었어 용서해줘
english: I didn't mean what I said back then. Please forgive me.
alt: What I said wasn't how I truly feel. I'm sorry.
note: didn't mean it = 진심이 아니었다 / forgive = 용서하다

korean: 숙면하려면 방 온도가 중요해
english: Room temperature is important for quality sleep.
alt: Keep your bedroom cool for the best sleep.
note: room temperature = 방 온도 / quality sleep = 숙면

korean: 잠들기 전에 따뜻한 우유 마시면 좋아
english: Drinking warm milk before bed helps you sleep.
alt: A glass of warm milk at bedtime works wonders.
note: warm milk = 따뜻한 우유 / bedtime = 취침 시간

korean: 퇴근하면 아무 생각도 하고 싶지 않아
english: After work I don't want to think about anything.
alt: When I clock out my brain shuts down completely.
note: clock out = 퇴근하다 / shuts down = 멈추다

korean: 스트레스 받으면 과식해 못 참겠어
english: When I'm stressed I overeat. I can't help it.
alt: Stress triggers my binge eating. I lose all control.
note: overeat = 과식하다 / binge eating = 폭식

korean: 건강한 간식으로 바꿔보려고 견과류 사왔어
english: I bought nuts to switch to healthier snacks.
alt: Trying to swap junk food for nuts and healthy alternatives.
note: nuts = 견과류 / healthier = 더 건강한

korean: 식단 기록하면 뭘 먹었는지 보여서 도움 돼
english: Logging your meals shows you what you ate so it's helpful.
alt: Food journaling helps you stay accountable with your diet.
note: logging meals = 식단 기록 / food journaling = 식단 일기

korean: 자막 한글로 바꿔줘 영어 자막 못 읽겠어
english: Switch the subtitles to Korean. I can't read the English ones.
alt: Change subs to Korean please. English is too fast for me.
note: subtitles = 자막 / switch = 바꾸다

korean: 이 감독 전작도 재밌었어 기대돼
english: This director's previous work was great too. I have high hopes.
alt: If it's from the same director I'm already sold.
note: director = 감독 / previous work = 전작

korean: 이 노래 라이브 버전이 음원보다 나아
english: The live version of this song is better than the studio version.
alt: Live recordings hit different. Way better than the album cut.
note: live version = 라이브 버전 / studio version = 음원

korean: 음악 취향이 비슷해서 플리 교환하자
english: We have similar music taste so let's swap playlists.
alt: Our taste in music lines up perfectly. Let's share playlists.
note: music taste = 음악 취향 / swap = 교환하다

korean: 인디 게임 중에 숨은 명작이 많아
english: There are a lot of hidden gems among indie games.
alt: Indie games have some seriously underrated masterpieces.
note: indie games = 인디 게임 / hidden gems = 숨은 명작

korean: 게임 패드 사야겠다 키보드로는 불편해
english: I should buy a gamepad. The keyboard is uncomfortable.
alt: Need a controller. Playing with keyboard and mouse isn't cutting it.
note: gamepad = 게임 패드 / controller = 컨트롤러

korean: 책 읽다가 잠들었어 어디까지 읽었는지 모르겠어
english: I fell asleep while reading and I don't know where I left off.
alt: Dozed off with the book in my hands. Lost my page.
note: fell asleep = 잠들다 / left off = 멈춘 곳

korean: 이 책 대출 기간 지났어 연장해야 해
english: This book is overdue. I need to renew it.
alt: My library book is past due. Better extend the loan.
note: overdue = 기간 지난 / renew = 연장하다

korean: 된장국 끓일 때 애호박 넣으면 맛있어
english: Adding zucchini when making doenjang soup makes it delicious.
alt: Throw in some zucchini for your doenjang-guk. Game changer.
note: doenjang soup = 된장국 / zucchini = 애호박

korean: 전자레인지로 데워먹으면 편해
english: Microwaving leftovers is convenient.
alt: Just nuke it in the microwave. Quick and easy.
note: microwave = 전자레인지 / nuke = 전자레인지에 돌리다 (구어)

korean: 걸레 빨아야 하는데 냄새 나 새 걸로 사야겠다
english: The mop cloth needs washing but it smells. I'll just buy a new one.
alt: This mop cloth reeks. Time to replace it.
note: mop cloth = 걸레 / reeks = 냄새나다

korean: 에어컨 필터 청소 안 하면 먼지가 나와
english: If you don't clean the AC filter dust comes out.
alt: Dirty AC filters blow dust all over the room.
note: AC filter = 에어컨 필터 / dust = 먼지

korean: 이사 갈 때 인터넷 이전 신청해야 해
english: When moving I need to apply for internet relocation.
alt: Don't forget to transfer your internet service to the new address.
note: internet relocation = 인터넷 이전 / transfer = 이전하다

korean: 월세 계약서 꼼꼼히 읽어봐야 해
english: You need to read the rental contract carefully.
alt: Go over the lease agreement with a fine-tooth comb.
note: rental contract = 월세 계약서 / lease agreement = 임대 계약서

korean: 강아지 목욕시키려니까 도망가
english: My dog runs away when I try to give it a bath.
alt: The pup bolts every time I mention bath time.
note: runs away = 도망가다 / bolts = 도망치다

korean: 고양이 앞발로 꾹꾹이 하는 거 너무 귀여워
english: My cat kneading with its front paws is so cute.
alt: When the cat does the kneading thing I just melt.
note: kneading = 꾹꾹이 / melt = 녹다 (감동)

korean: 팔굽혀펴기 20개도 힘들어 체력이 바닥이야
english: I can barely do 20 push-ups. My stamina is at rock bottom.
alt: Twenty push-ups nearly killed me. I'm so out of shape.
note: push-ups = 팔굽혀펴기 / out of shape = 체력이 안 좋은

korean: 운동복 새로 사야겠다 다 늘어났어
english: I need new workout clothes. Everything is stretched out.
alt: My gym gear is all worn out. Time for new stuff.
note: workout clothes = 운동복 / stretched out = 늘어난

korean: 뿌리 염색 해야 해 새치가 보여
english: I need to touch up my roots. The grays are showing.
alt: My roots are showing. Time for a color touch-up.
note: touch up roots = 뿌리 염색 / grays = 새치

korean: 보습 마스크팩 하니까 피부가 촉촉해졌어
english: My skin feels so hydrated after using a moisturizing sheet mask.
alt: A hydrating mask did wonders for my dry skin.
note: sheet mask = 마스크팩 / hydrated = 촉촉한

korean: 유턴할 데가 없어 돌아가야 해
english: There's no place to make a U-turn. I have to go around.
alt: Can't U-turn here. Gotta take the long way around.
note: U-turn = 유턴 / go around = 돌아가다

korean: 네비가 자꾸 다른 길로 안내해
english: The GPS keeps navigating to the wrong route.
alt: My nav keeps rerouting me somewhere weird.
note: GPS = 네비 / rerouting = 다시 안내하는

korean: 와이파이 통화 써봐 전화비 안 나와
english: Try Wi-Fi calling. It doesn't cost anything.
alt: Use Wi-Fi calling. It's completely free.
note: Wi-Fi calling = 와이파이 통화 / free = 무료

korean: 전화 오면 받아줘 내가 지금 운전 중이야
english: Answer the phone if it rings. I'm driving right now.
alt: Pick up any calls for me. I'm behind the wheel.
note: answer = 받다 / behind the wheel = 운전 중인

korean: 교환하려는데 같은 사이즈 재고 있어요?
english: I want to exchange this. Do you have the same size in stock?
alt: Is this available in the same size? I'd like to swap it.
note: exchange = 교환 / in stock = 재고 있는

korean: 선착순 한정 수량이래 빨리 주문해
english: It's first come first served and limited quantity. Order fast.
alt: Limited stock going fast. Better order now before it sells out.
note: first come first served = 선착순 / limited quantity = 한정 수량

korean: 소액결제 한도 늘려야겠다
english: I need to increase my micro-payment limit.
alt: Gotta raise my small transaction cap.
note: micro-payment = 소액결제 / limit = 한도

korean: 배당금 들어왔어 기분 좋다
english: The dividend came in. Feeling good.
alt: Got my dividend payment today. Nice little bonus.
note: dividend = 배당금 / bonus = 보너스

korean: 엑스레이 찍었는데 뼈에는 이상 없대
english: I got an X-ray and they said my bones are fine.
alt: X-ray came back clear. No fractures.
note: X-ray = 엑스레이 / fractures = 골절

korean: 건강보험료 인상됐어 부담이야
english: Health insurance premiums went up. It's a burden.
alt: The insurance rate hike is hitting my wallet hard.
note: health insurance = 건강보험 / premiums = 보험료

korean: 크로플 먹어봤어? 여기 맛있대
english: Have you tried a croffle? This place is supposed to be good.
alt: They say the croffles here are amazing. Wanna try?
note: croffle = 크로플 (크로아상 와플)

korean: 오늘 카페 사장님이 서비스로 쿠키 줬어
english: The cafe owner gave me a cookie on the house today.
alt: Got a free cookie from the barista today. Made my day.
note: on the house = 서비스 / free = 무료

korean: 소주 한 병에 얼마예요?
english: How much is a bottle of soju?
alt: What's the price on a bottle of soju here?
note: bottle of soju = 소주 한 병

korean: 음주 후 해장은 라면이 최고야
english: Ramen is the best hangover cure after drinking.
alt: Nothing beats instant noodles for a post-drinking pick-me-up.
note: hangover cure = 해장 / pick-me-up = 기력 회복

korean: 엘리베이터에서 이웃 만나면 어색해
english: It's awkward running into neighbors in the elevator.
alt: Elevator small talk with neighbors is so uncomfortable.
note: awkward = 어색한 / running into = 마주치다

korean: 택배 배달원이 초인종 안 누르고 가
english: The delivery person leaves packages without ringing the bell.
alt: The courier never buzzes. Just drops and goes.
note: delivery person = 배달원 / buzzes = 초인종 누르다

korean: 습도가 높아서 빨래가 안 마른다
english: The humidity is so high the laundry won't dry.
alt: Nothing dries in this humidity. The clothes are still damp.
note: humidity = 습도 / damp = 축축한

korean: 단풍 들었다 드라이브 가기 딱 좋은 날씨야
english: The leaves have changed. Perfect weather for a drive.
alt: Fall colors are peaking. Ideal day for a scenic drive.
note: leaves have changed = 단풍 들었다 / scenic drive = 경치 좋은 드라이브

korean: 명절에 교통사고 조심해 안전운전 해
english: Be careful of traffic accidents during holidays. Drive safely.
alt: Drive safe during the holidays. Accidents spike this time of year.
note: traffic accidents = 교통사고 / drive safely = 안전운전

korean: 세뱃돈 모아서 뭐 살까 고민이야
english: I'm thinking about what to buy with my saved New Year's money.
alt: Saved up all my sebae money. Now what should I splurge on?
note: saved = 모은 / splurge = 크게 쓰다

korean: 합격 소식 듣고 너무 기뻐 축하해!
english: I'm so happy to hear you passed! Congratulations!
alt: That's the best news! So proud of you. Congrats!
note: passed = 합격 / so proud = 자랑스러운

korean: 이번 프로젝트 성공 축하해 고생 많았어
english: Congratulations on the project's success. You worked so hard.
alt: The project turned out great. Well done to everyone involved.
note: success = 성공 / well done = 수고했어

korean: 기분 나쁘게 했으면 사과할게
english: If I offended you I apologize.
alt: I'm sorry if anything I said came across wrong.
note: offended = 기분 나쁘게 하다 / came across wrong = 잘못 전달되다

korean: 어제 술 마시고 실수한 거 미안해
english: I'm sorry for what I did after drinking last night.
alt: Sorry about last night. I had too much and made a fool of myself.
note: made a fool of myself = 실수하다 / too much = 너무 많이

korean: 꿈에서 깨면 다시 잠들기 어려워
english: Once I wake up from a dream it's hard to fall back asleep.
alt: When a dream wakes me up I just lie there unable to drift off.
note: fall back asleep = 다시 잠들다 / drift off = 잠들다

korean: 인간관계 스트레스가 제일 힘들어
english: Relationship stress is the hardest to deal with.
alt: People problems stress me out more than anything else.
note: relationship stress = 인간관계 스트레스

korean: 폭식하면 죄책감 들어서 더 스트레스야
english: Binge eating makes me feel guilty which causes more stress.
alt: After a binge the guilt makes everything worse.
note: binge eating = 폭식 / guilty = 죄책감

korean: 이 영화 평점 높은데 정말 그만큼 재밌어?
english: This movie has high ratings but is it really that good?
alt: The ratings are sky-high. Does it actually live up to the hype?
note: ratings = 평점 / live up to the hype = 기대에 부응하다

korean: 아이돌 신곡 나왔어 뮤비 봤어?
english: The idol group dropped a new single. Have you seen the MV?
alt: New drop from my fave group. Watched the music video yet?
note: new single = 신곡 / MV = 뮤직비디오

korean: 이 게임 튜토리얼이 너무 길어 빨리 본편 하고 싶어
english: The tutorial in this game is way too long. I want to get to the main story.
alt: This tutorial drags on forever. Just let me play the actual game.
note: tutorial = 튜토리얼 / main story = 본편

korean: 이 소설 번역이 좀 어색해 원서로 읽을까
english: The translation of this novel is a bit awkward. Should I read the original?
alt: The translation feels off. Maybe I should try the original text.
note: translation = 번역 / original = 원서

korean: 고기 굽는 타이밍이 중요해 너무 익히면 질겨
english: Timing is key when grilling meat. Overcook it and it gets tough.
alt: Don't overdo the meat on the grill. It'll turn rubbery.
note: timing = 타이밍 / overcook = 너무 익히다 / tough = 질긴

korean: 싱크대 기름때 빼기 진짜 힘들다
english: Getting grease off the sink is so hard.
alt: Kitchen grease is a nightmare to scrub off.
note: grease = 기름때 / scrub off = 문질러 닦다

korean: 이사하면 주소 변경 신고해야 돼 은행이랑 우체국도
english: After moving you need to update your address with the bank and post office too.
alt: Don't forget to change your address everywhere after the move.
note: update address = 주소 변경 / post office = 우체국

korean: 강아지 간식 너무 많이 주면 살쪄 조절해
english: Don't give your dog too many treats or it'll gain weight.
alt: Watch the treat portions. Overfeeding leads to a chubby pup.
note: treats = 간식 / gain weight = 살찌다

korean: 유산소 하기 싫으면 걷기라도 해
english: If you don't want to do cardio at least go for a walk.
alt: Even a walk counts as exercise. Just get moving.
note: cardio = 유산소 / go for a walk = 걷기

korean: 피부 타입 모르면 피부과에서 상담 받아봐
english: If you don't know your skin type consult a dermatologist.
alt: Not sure about your skin type? A dermatologist can figure it out.
note: skin type = 피부 타입 / consult = 상담하다

korean: 교통카드 찍는 거 깜빡해서 벌금 낼 뻔했어
english: I almost got fined because I forgot to tap my transit card.
alt: Nearly got caught without tapping my card. That was close.
note: tap = 찍다 / fined = 벌금

korean: 받는 분 성함이랑 연락처 알려주세요
english: Please tell me the recipient's name and contact number.
alt: I'll need the receiver's name and phone number.
note: recipient = 받는 사람 / contact number = 연락처

korean: 이체 한도 초과했어 내일 보내줄게
english: I exceeded the transfer limit. I'll send it tomorrow.
alt: Hit my daily transfer cap. I'll wire it first thing tomorrow.
note: transfer limit = 이체 한도 / exceeded = 초과하다

korean: CT 찍어야 한대 검사 결과 나오려면 며칠 걸려
english: They say I need a CT scan. It takes a few days for results.
alt: Got to get a CT. Results won't be ready for a couple of days.
note: CT scan = CT 촬영 / results = 결과

korean: 이 카페 디카페인 종류가 많아서 좋아
english: I love that this cafe has a wide selection of decaf options.
alt: This place has so many decaf choices. Perfect for evening visits.
note: decaf = 디카페인 / selection = 종류

korean: 건배사 누가 할 거야? 네가 해
english: Who's giving the toast? You do it.
alt: Someone needs to make a toast. You're up.
note: toast = 건배사 / you're up = 네 차례야

korean: 낙엽 밟는 소리가 좋아 가을이 좋아
english: I love the sound of stepping on fallen leaves. I love autumn.
alt: The crunch of autumn leaves underfoot is the best.
note: fallen leaves = 낙엽 / crunch = 바스락 소리

korean: 명절에 고향 못 가면 서운해
english: It's sad when I can't go to my hometown during holidays.
alt: Missing the holiday hometown trip always bums me out.
note: hometown = 고향 / bums me out = 서운하다

korean: 쌍둥이야? 대박! 두 배로 축하해!
english: Twins? Wow! Double the congratulations!
alt: Twins?! That's twice the joy! Congrats times two!
note: twins = 쌍둥이 / double = 두 배

korean: 네 입장에서 생각 못 한 거 미안해
english: I'm sorry I didn't consider your perspective.
alt: I should have seen it from your point of view. My bad.
note: perspective = 입장 / point of view = 관점

korean: 잠자리가 바뀌면 첫날은 잠이 잘 안 와
english: I have trouble sleeping the first night in a new bed.
alt: Sleeping in an unfamiliar bed always messes up my first night.
note: trouble sleeping = 잠이 안 오다 / unfamiliar = 낯선

korean: 스트레스 받으면 단 거 말고 차라리 운동해
english: When you're stressed exercise instead of reaching for sweets.
alt: Swap stress snacking for a workout. It helps way more.
note: exercise = 운동 / reaching for = ~에 손 뻗다
